programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Part two of two. Mark recalls an incident from 20 years previously that appears to hold the key to solving the case involving cover-ups, radioactive fuel and the death of an airline rep. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A TV executive is murdered and the subsequent death of the chief suspect makes Mark and Steve realise a serial killer is on the loose. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: A man who knew Richard Kimble as Eddie Fry, one of his many identities in the past, meets Kimble again and gives him some startling news.

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: A young woman unknowingly signs a modelling contract that allows her agent to take half her money, and when the agent is found dead, she's accused of murder

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker is left for dead after a nasty confrontation with two cattle ranchers, who attempted to kill a herd of federally protected wild mustangs grazing on their property

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A prison bus is involved in an accident and several inmates take advantage of the confusion to make good their escape, including Horatio's son Kyle. Desperate to stop them, Tripp resorts to using his gun - but when the smoke clears, an innocent bystander has been hit. Starring David Caruso

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Director Vance charges DiNozzo with a special assignment to go to France, locate Admiral Kendall's wayward daughter Amanda and bring her home. Arriving in Marseille, Tony immediately heads to the local NCIS office, only to find five dead agents. After eventually tracking down Amanda, the pair embark on a rollercoaster ride to the relative safety of the nearest NCIS office in Spain. Michael Weatherly stars

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: An attack on a private plane immediately after it lands turns out to be the hijack of the secret army transfer of terrorist leader Ala A Din Keshwar, the man whose release was demanded in exchange for Dom's life. Callen and the rest of the NCIS team vow to get their hands on Keshwar before he can flee the country. Meanwhile, Hetty offers her resignation to Vance following Dom's death

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: The tables are turned when Gerard is falsely accused of a crime and sentenced to death in a mountain community which holds itself beyond the law

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Perry defends a young engineer accused of murdering his boss, but the investigation leads to the discovery of another crime that has been hidden for years

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Against his better judgement, Gibbs comes out of retirement to help Fornell and his daughter, whose lives are being threatened by an escaped convict. The team reopens the criminal's case and questions a former sailor, the only person to have contacted him while he was in jail. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette, with Hal Holbrook

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker's attempt to rescue a baby trapped inside a car dangling from a bridge leaves him in a coma. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents reluctantly team up with the coast guard to investigate the apparent murder of a Navy diver who was trying to recover a sunken ship and its treasure, and Gibbs finds a prime suspect in a wealthy doctor who had bankrolled the underwater mission. Starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen's cover is blown while he is working undercover and he sends a coded text to Hetty, who effectively cuts him off from NCIS as she makes it the team's top priority to discover who found out his real identity and how. A man called Eugene Keelson contacts Callen and threatens to reveal information about his past unless he runs an errand for him, which involves delivering a flash drive to Bulgarian gang. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A missing US Navy lieutenant's car is found covered in blood, and the team discovers she had been profiling men to find the perfect partner. programme.desc: Gibbs travels to his home town after hearing news of his father's death, while the team investigates whether a fire on a US Navy ship was accidental or planned. The vessel served as prison to several members of the Brotherhood of Doubt terrorist group, whom Gibbs was responsible for catching. After the blaze, two of those members are missing. Tony argues that Gibbs would want to know, but Vance does not want him disturbed at this difficult time. Mark Harmon stars

Starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Former EastEnders actress Lucy Speed joins the cast as DC Stevie Moss, who brings a breath of fresh air to CID with her positive and lively outlook - though she manages to upset Mickey on her first day by stealing his parking space. Her first case involves a professional footballer who is forced to admit he is gay after his closet lover is run over in a hit-and-run incident involving his fiancee. Suspicion falls on the player's manager Ernie Rosen, who also happens to be his prospective father-in-law and anxious to avoid the shame of a scandal in the family, but Sally uncovers evidence to suggest he is covering for someone even closer to home

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: In light of his recent diagnosis, Ric struggles to concentrate on preparing a presentation for the board, and asks Jac to manage a transplant for him. Penny covers for Frieda when she cuts corners and treats a patient herself instead of calling a doctor for assistance, and Greg resents his appointment as Oliver's new mentor

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Nina receives a blast from the past when a familiar face turns up, and Ellen soon realises the woman in question was more than just a friend. An angry Sam refuses to heed Fleur's advice to stay away from Comfort. Selena plots revenge on Will as she returns to work, and Harry encourages a patient to sue when Jas misdiagnoses his condition. Medical drama, starring Rebekah Gibbs

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A student reports being attacked in the park on her way home from the bar where she works, but a history of false allegations casts doubt on her story until the Sun Hill watch realise they have a dangerous and increasingly reckless maniac on their hands. Kezia recognises a familiar face on the list of suspects but, in the absence of evidence, Sally agrees to go undercover to catch the culprit. David Sibley guest stars

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian tells David he knows of his affair with Cindy. Frankie is causing trouble between Alan and Carol

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian is determined to find out truth about Cindy and David. While Tony is convinced of his feelings for Simon, he is less certain of how to face the rest of Albert Square

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: The lads receive an emergency call while on jungle manoeuvres Down Under, and rush off to Hope Bay, the village adjacent to their camp, which has just been devastated by a cyclone. Taylor manfully puts affairs of the heart on hold to supervise rescue operations, but unfortunately, not everyone can be saved

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James manages to arrange a date with Helen after she visits the surgery with her dog Dan, while Tristan runs into problems with an awkward customer and Siegfried's temper fails to improve when he tries to give up smoking

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The dodgy dealer is asked to furnish a restaurant, but the items he acquires turn out to be stolen. To make matters worse, the owners turn up for the establishment's opening night. John Wells guest stars, with Ian McShane

programme.title: Brush Strokes
programme.desc: Jacko finally realises what the missing piece of the jigsaw is in his life - but he faces a race against time to avoid missing out on it. Elmo plans a fresh start in Australia

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Mr Humphries catches a rare and contagious tropical disease - and it is not long before his workmates are getting on each other's nerves even more than usual while quarantined from the outside world. Vintage comedy, starring Mollie Sugden, John Inman and Frank Thornton

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: The trio decide there is profit to be made from a heatwave, so Seymour designs a vendor's side car cooler to put Compo into business as an ice-cream man. Comedy, starring Michael Aldridge and Bill Owen

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo and Clegg hatch a devious plan to free henpecked Wally and Howard from their wives - if only for a few hours. Bill Owen and Peter Sallis star

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: A prostitute turns up at the station claiming to have been raped, and as Gently looks back at past cases, he is shocked to discover that very few have been successfully prosecuted - if they made it to court in the first place. Risking animosity from his colleagues, he becomes suspicious when he notices the same police inspector's name appearing repeatedly in the files. To complicate matters, the body of another rape victim is found, prompting a visit to the local brothel. Meanwhile, Bacchus has started an affair with Gemma, the wife of a fellow copper. Martin Shaw, Lee Ingleby and Lisa McGrillis star, with guest appearances from Denise Welch, Derek Riddell and Emily Woof

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: In a special edition of the detective drama that features only two members of the team, Gerry Standing and Steve McAndrew travel to Glasgow, where a new Ucos section is being set up. While there, they agree to assist the investigation into the 1993 unsolved murder of James Soutar, the wealthy owner of a string of betting shops. Cathy Sinclair, one of the beneficiaries of the bookie's will at his death, had a care-home upbringing in common with Soutar, but she claims to have no idea who he was or why she was left £15,000. McAndrew has a personal stake in the case - the original investigating officer was corrupt cop Frank McNair, who had an affair with his wife, and their paths soon cross. The London-based officers' questions begin to unsettle someone and a campaign of victimisation against them begins, but it's a revelation from McAndrew's girlfriend Charley that reawakens a long-dormant scandal and clears the path to a solution. Dennis Waterman and Denis Lawson star

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Leo and Harry are called to investigate the unexpected deaths of three patients on the same ward at a London hospital, and are introduced to DC Julia Catina and DI Frank Skipper. Suspicion falls on a professor, but when he commits suicide, Nikki comes under pressure from his daughter to find a reason for his actions, and the pathologist soon makes a link to a case involving rape and murder

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Penny makes a choice when Hugh and Toke turn up the romantic pressure. When Charlie is unfairly sacked by the Headmaster, Meryl resorts to blackmail to get her reinstated

programme.title: Mercy Street
programme.desc: After Antietam, the Greens suffer a setback and Pinkerton's inquiry heats up. Foster and Samuel visit Foster's family plantation, where they intervene in a difficult labour. The hospital staff unites to oust their hated chief. In Boston, Mary's condition worsens

programme.desc: Ian is determined to find out truth about Cindy and David. While Tony is convinced of his feelings for Simon, he is less certain of how to face the rest of Albert Square

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Sam's 19th birthday celebrations turn into a nightmare for Tess when a volatile Fleur takes over the arrangements. Woody grows suspicious of a new paramedic who seems unable to perform a simple procedure and Nina tells Abs about her affair with Army friend Sarah. Jas warns Guppy never to contact his parents again, while Charlie announces he is taking a sabbatical. Medical drama, starring Suzanne Packer

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. An 11-year-old boy is abducted by a hooded man in a shopping centre, leading to fears a copycat child killer is on the loose in Sun Hill. Mike Wilson becomes prime suspect when a shrine to convicted murderer Linda Johnson and her deceased partner in crime is found at his home, but as Neil grows closer to his informant in her cell he discovers the case is not nearly as straightforward as it first appeared

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Cindy leaves Ian, forcing Ian to confront David. Meanwhile, Joe is still acting strangely, Gita wants another baby, and Tony tells Sarah he's gay

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Alan is feeling guilty about Frankie. Joe is still behaving strangely. Ian challenges Cindy at the Court hearing

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: Dave and Donna's relationship is threatened, and Paddy Garvey and Nancy Thorpe make some important decisions. Starring Robson Green and Jerome Flynn

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James's luck takes a sudden turn for the better in his romance with Helen, but there is no respite for the unfortunate Dalby family. Tristan's capacity for causing chaos shows no sign of coming to an end

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The antiques dealer is asked to track down an itinerant painter who holds the key to a shameful secret. Light-hearted drama, starring Ian McShane

programme.title: Brush Strokes
programme.desc: Jacko is off the hook when Lucia's father reveals that she is marrying someone else, and Elmo's golddigging girlfriend puts pressure on him to buy a big house in the country

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Captain Peacock's wife is hired as Mr Rumbold's secretary, and he becomes convinced the two are having an adulterous liaison. Comedy, starring Frank Thornton, Nicholas Smith and Diana King

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: After Clegg is visited by three teenage missionaries, Seymour believes it is about time he offered his considerable talents and services to the local vicar, so sets about selling tickets for its staging of The Tales of Beatrix Potter. Peter Sallis and Michael Aldridge star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Seymour vows that the days of Edie having to carry a dustbin are over, and promises to install a waste disposal unit for her. Meanwhile, a former wartime comrade of Wally's makes an appearance. Michael Aldridge, Thora Hird and Joe Gladwin star

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Lynley returns to his ancestral home to celebrate his engagement, but 20 years of anger over his mother's indiscretion with a doctor makes the occasion unpleasant. A murder in a nearby village provides him with a temporary distraction - until mounting evidence appears to suggest his brother is implicated. Mystery drama, starring Nathaniel Parker

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Strickland's Whitehall pal Stephen Fisher is targeted by an assassin, so the Ucos boss enlists the team to find whoever was responsible. He reveals that 30 years earlier, he and Fisher were involved in a covert MI5 operation to break into the house of a journalist believed to have information on an IRA arms deals. But with several of the original agents having died in suspicious circumstances, he believes someone is out to kill them all - and when a connection is made between a gangster and MI5, it seems the threat may be coming from within the security service itself. Tim McInnerny and Kerry Fox guest star

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A preserved female body is found buried in a Sheffield peat moor, and DI Sonia Hardwick calls in archaeologist Patrick Cain to help with the investigation, assisted by Nikki. When an examination of the corpse suggests a sexually motivated attack, Leo suspects there is a link to a serial killer case he dealt with in 1985, but following the lead means returning to his home town and confronting the memories of his dead wife and child. Guest starring Mark Benton and Barbara Flynn, with William Gaminara

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. An 11-year-old boy is abducted by a hooded man in a shopping centre, leading to fears a copycat child killer is on the loose in Sun Hill. Mike Wilson becomes prime suspect when a shrine to convicted murderer Linda Johnson and her deceased partner in crime is found at his home, but as Neil grows closer to his informant in her cell he discovers the case is not nearly as straightforward as it first appeared

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Hugh struggles to prove his innocence and win back Penny after failing a drug test. Meryl and Charlie lock horns over Hayley's entry in the Miss Whyhope competition

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Monarch of the Glen
programme.desc: Archie has to choose between giving his business partnership with Paul one last chance or going mountaineering in Nepal with his sister Lizzie to honour their father's memory. Meanwhile, Molly is angry that Andrew has ignored her since returning from their romantic weekend, but Golly gains the courage to admit his hidden feelings to her. Alastair Mackenzie and Susan Hampshire star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. An 11-year-old boy is abducted by a hooded man in a shopping centre, leading to fears a copycat child killer is on the loose in Sun Hill. Mike Wilson becomes prime suspect when a shrine to convicted murderer Linda Johnson and her deceased partner in crime is found at his home, but as Neil grows closer to his informant in her cell he discovers the case is not nearly as straightforward as it first appeared

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Michael's one-night stand is rushed into AAU after overdosing on cocaine, and Ric realises pursuing his relationship with Annalese will only intensify his colleague's erratic behaviour. Meanwhile, Frieda jeopardises her professional reputation to take revenge on a racist patient, and Oliver worries he might have blown his chance to impress Greg when he makes a bad error of judgement

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Fleur's devious and deranged behaviour reaches crisis point as a worried Abs turns up at Tess's house. The volatile youngster grabs a knife and barricades herself in the bedroom with Sam when Sarah and Nina arrive on the scene. Meanwhile, Woody contacts Susan, who says there is a chance Matt is not his son. Guppy tries to speak to his father and Josh is appalled by conditions at the hospital

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Heaton calls a press conference to tackle the growing media interest in Linda Johnson's involvement in the Clark abduction case, unwittingly attracting the attention of her victims' relatives as well. A ransom note mysteriously appears at the station demanding Linda drop off £10,000 in the park to secure Dean's release, but the ensuing sting operation threatens to end in tragedy

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: When Phil comes home from Scotland the welcome is not as warm as he'd like. Cindy has a frustrating day without David. Alan is in deep water, while Robbie talks to the press

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ted and Kathy have a heart to heart. Mark does some babysitting and Simon starts a new job. Emotional turmoil drives Cindy to desperate acts, while Roy plans his marriage to Pat

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: Brad Connor is devastated to learn he may be HIV positive, and makes a drunken pass at Marsha Stubbs. Joy Wilton discovers the joys of mixing work with pleasure

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: The Dalbys' long spell of misfortune comes to an end as James stumbles on the cause of the disease afflicting the family's herd. Mrs Hall advises James to get on with it vis-a-vis Helen and warns him that he risks losing her, with Richard Edmundson still vying for her affections. James finally pops the question, but shudders at the thought of having to ask Mr Alderson's permission

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: An ageing film star decides to sell some antiques, much to the delight of Tinker, who is a big fan - but her house is robbed just before the auction. Ian McShane stars

programme.title: Brush Strokes
programme.desc: Veronica takes over running Lionel's company, and rehires Sandra and Jacko, who finds himself having a run-in with the law. Elmo uses his newfound wealth to open a wine bar

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Grace Brothers decides to start an in-house magazine with Mr Humphries as the editor, but the new publication causes rifts among members of the staff. Vintage comedy, starring John Inman, Mollie Sugden and Wendy Richard

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Having witnessed his sister Edie fail several driving tests, Seymour gallantly volunteers to help teach her. However, because he is unable to control a vehicle himself, he needs a qualified motorist to ride along. Michael Aldridge and Thora Hird star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Seymour comes up with another madcap scheme to harness the power of nature using an old sail, a pair of roller skates and, in Compo, a very gullible 'test pilot'. Michael Aldridge, Bill Owen and Peter Sallis star

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: A youngster refuses to undergo a life-saving transplant operation, much to the dismay of his parents, who try to overrule him by taking the matter to court - even though he meets all the legal requirements to make his own decision. In a separate case, two drug addicts accused of killing a boy for his mobile phone are found not guilty of murder

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Part one of two. Brian is suspended after assaulting a high-ranking officer in the Met, suspecting him of covering up a death in custody years before. Meanwhile, an Argentinian pistol recovered from the Thames is linked to two unsolved murders which took place 16 years apart - and with one of them having occurred in Gibraltar, Sandra takes the team - minus Brian - on an Iberian adventure to uncover the truth. Alun Armstrong, Amanda Redman, Dennis Waterman and Denis Lawson star in the crime drama

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Nikki is called to perform a post mortem on the body of a woman found in a river, and the discovery that she had recently given birth causes the pathologist to worry her child may be abandoned somewhere. Meanwhile, Harry investigates the apparent suicide of a lieutenant at a nearby Army base, and suspicions soon arise that the two cases may be linked. Starring Emilia Fox and Tom Ward

programme.title: EastEnders

Kim plays matchmaker for Peter and Ash, and despite the latter's reluctance they decide to go on a date - only for Suki to dampen the mood. Martin is concerned when Ruby makes it clear she wants to move on, and a talent scout appears in Walford

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?

Comedy legend Dawn French joins Michelle Visage and Alan Carr on the judging panel as another queen takes her leave…

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Line of Duty stars Vicky McClure and Adrian Dunbar talk about the hotly awaited sixth series of the BBC's crime drama. Star Wars' Daisy Ridley chats about starring in sci-fi adventure Chaos Walking, Breaking Bad's Bryan Cranston reflects on his role as a New Orleans judge in the TV drama Your Honor and Brit star Chiwetel Ejiofor promotes his romantic comedy heist, Locked Down. Plus, Imelda May performs Made to Love

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley present appeals for help with unsolved cases. Reporter John-Paul Davies is out and about with stories from across the UK

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Rob tries to get away from it all with a day of fishing only to be interrupted by an unexpected arrival. Ruhma is surprised to find out why an anxious new dad-to-be is showing such a high level of interest in his wife's pregnancy. Daniel is invited to meet the deputy vice chancellor of the university

programme.title: Moving On

The presenter also visits a quarry from which Cotswold stone is used for new builds and conservation projects

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Mother and daughter Jo and Millie from Berwick-upon-Tweed run a food waste cafe. Today they are looking for new ways to use the food that is most often donated. Ellis Barrie and Romy Gill come up with a couple of ideas, while Rylan Clark-Neal keeps things ticking over

programme.title: The Bidding Room

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ash and Peter agree to try again after their disastrous date, sharing a passionate kiss just as Iqra arrives. Lucas overhears Chelsea arguing with Jack and accuses his daughter of bullying but she reminds him that if the job goes wrong, it is her life on the line. Later, Lucas makes a date with Karen - but doesn't give her his real name

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Chloe is suspended, unable to explain why one of her patients died - and as she leaves she confides her suspicion to Ange that Cameron was involved in the patient's death. Sahira considers bumping her father up the transplant list and on Lucky's last day she pulls out all the stops to help a patient with a phobia of hospitals. Will it be enough for Max to keep the psychiatric nurse on at Holby?

programme.title: The Royle Family
programme.desc: The morning of the wedding arrives and it is not just Denise's nerves that are on edge. Jim has diarrhoea and a constipated Nana warns the Royle bride that it's all downhill from here

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Having spent the night with Bradley, a contented Norman returns home to find that his mother is in jail, arrested for the murder of Keith Summers. Her bail is set at $100,000, but she refuses her sons' offer to help her. Norman puts up the hotel as collateral for a bond, but Norma tries to make him feel guilty about his not being there when she was arrested

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Dylan tells Gil what happened to Ethan and gets a new partner - Remo - while Norma has a hard time accepting that Shelby kept Jiao a prisoner in his basement. Emma wants to call the FBI immediately to tell them about the sex ring, but Norma wants to get Keith Summers' belt from Shelby before they do anything

However, his new hobby threatens to drive a wedge between him and his close-knit group of friends. Starring Les Dennis and Liz McClarnon, with Aaron Cobham, Louis Emerick and Michael Starke

programme.title: Escape to the Country

programme.title: Party Political Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Liberal Democrats

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: I Can't Say My Name: Stammering in the Spotlight
programme.desc: BBC producer Felicity Baker has a stammer that she has spent her whole life trying to hide. She struggles to say her own name. Now she is stepping out in front of the camera for the first time to talk about what it is like to live with the condition - and she soon finds she is not alone. She meets a rugby player who blames his stammer for his aggressive behaviour on the pitch, a rapper who found music stopped him stammering and the comedy star Michael Palin, whose father stammered but never ever spoke about it

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metal maestro Dominic Chinea gets to work on a crumbling cast-iron garden gate, whose design includes the badge of the Royal Lancers, while horologist Steve fletcher and leather expert Suzie Fletcher join up to restore a wristwatch awarded for bravery in the First World War. Upholsterer Sonnaz Nooranvary takes on a rocking chair that has been at the heart of a family home for decades, and Geoff Harvey is given a 1920s penny arcade game

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Bank Balance
programme.desc: Gordon welcomes two more pairs - partners Caroline and Andrew, and brothers Sam and Sassan. Will either duo have what it takes to beat the board and walk away with the jackpot of £100,000?

programme.title: Zara McDermott: Revenge Porn
programme.desc: Zara McDermott shot to fame on Love Island in 2018. But as soon as she left the villa, Zara was told that naked photos she had shared with an ex-lover had started circulating worldwide. The incident left Zara devastated, ashamed and looking for answers. Here she sets out to understand more about the impact of revenge porn on victims and questions whether anyone can safely share intimate images again

programme.title: Catch Her If You Can
programme.desc: Documentary following an attempt to expose a successful London businesswoman's double life as a con artist. A suspicious invoice tipped off a potential victim, and inspired her to track down other parties who had been fleeced in the past. Together, they came up with a plan to expose the woman's misdeeds

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste report on a woman suffering from what she felt was a type of Stockholm syndrome, in that she was unable to escape a relationship with a man who took tens of thousands of pounds from her

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don and the team look back at some of the floral highlights of 2020. It is a celebration of some of the star flowers of gardens, from tulips and roses to salvias and dahlias. There is also another chance to see what viewers were getting up to in their own gardens throughout the year

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: Alicia McCarthy presents a round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: Mark Wright demonstrates exercises designed to get muscles moving, and is joined by singer-songwriter and footballer Chelcee Grimes

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Bitesize: 11-12 Year Olds
programme.desc: Reflections in maths, the Suffragettes, and Jodie Kidd reading Journey to the River Sea.

programme.title: Bitesize: 12-13 Year Olds
programme.desc: Sensory language, and Dina Asher-Smith reads from Noughts and Crosses by Malorie Blackman

programme.title: Bitesize: 13-14 Year Olds
programme.desc: The Suffragettes, enlargement of shapes, and writing techniques

programme.title: 1066: A Year to Conquer England
programme.desc: Docudrama in which Dan Snow explores the political intrigues and family betrayals between Vikings, Anglo-Saxons and Normans that led to the Battle of Hastings. The first edition reveals how the death of Edward the Confessor without an heir triggered a bitter race to succeed him as king of England, with Earl Harold successfully taking the crown. However, William of Normandy believes the throne has been promised to him and decides to invade, while Viking king Harald Hardrada also fancies himself as monarch

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Robshaw family experience life at the turn of the 20th century, and discover how the food people ate and the way they consumed it helped shape the modern family. An ordinary house in South London will be their time machine, transporting them through five decades and two world wars. Guided by presenters Giles Coren and social historian Polly Russell, they'll trace the changes to Britain's diet and the extraordinary social transformation they reveal. Monica Galetti and Chas and Dave make guest appearances

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Homeowners in York open their doors to five neighbours, featuring a Victorian cottage, a 1960s farmhouse given a new lease of life and a 1980s bungalow

programme.title: The Wonder of Animals
programme.desc: Wildlife documentary making use of scientific research into animal anatomy to explain how various species are uniquely built to thrive in specific habitats. Chris Packham begins by examine details the penguin, detailing how the aquatic, flightless bird's legs, wings and body shape have allowed it to conquer a wide range of landscapes, from deep forests and tropical waters to bustling cities and the toughest place on the planet - Antarctica

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A collection of interesting and previously unseen finds from the show, with visits to Margam Country Park in south Wales, Highcliffe Castle in Dorset and Powderham Castle in Devon

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Mina Anwar, Glen Moore, Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Shaun Williamson go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon Parfett and his team head to the Black Mountains in south-east Wales to help Alex and Laura finish building a tiny house on wheels

programme.title: Mary Berry Everyday
programme.desc: The food writer and former Great British Bake Off judge continues her celebration of the everyday food and ingredients she has always loved to cook, and which have formed the cornerstone of her work for more than six decades. In this edition, Mary shares recipes for inspiring hearty dishes full of wholesome goodness, from her secret tip for a rich bolognese sauce to a soup that will warm the cockles. She also dons a white suit and tries her hand at beekeeping, collecting fresh honey to make a winning pudding

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contestants to brave the black chair, asking them questions on the specialist subjects of the Russian Revolution, King Crimson, The Hunger Games, and the essays and journalism of George Orwell, before they are tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell presents the first semi-final of the quiz as two teams compete to guess the connections between things that seem utterly random at first glance, with one set of clues asking what completes the sequence of Vijfhuizen: Houses, Sichuan: Rivers, and Tripoli: Cities

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Two teams of four students battle it out in the penultimate quarter-final, hoping to make it to the next stage of the competition as they try to win two matches and progress to the semi-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Miss World 1970: Beauty Queens and Bedlam
programme.desc: The story of the protest at the contest, when protestors dramatically disrupted the event and kickstarted a feminist revolution in front of an audience of millions. Hannah Berryman's film uses archive, animation and contributions from key players to offer an entertaining and thought-provoking celebration of a night of game-changing anarchy

programme.title: Funny Festival Live
programme.desc: Stand-up performances by established and up-and-coming comedians, recorded in front of a live virtual audience over three nights at a Covid-safe venue in south London

programme.title: Harry Hill's Lonely Island
programme.desc: Silent slapstick comedy short from Harry Hill

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous: The Movie (2016)
programme.desc: Worried about her finances when her book fails to find a publisher, Patsy is given the tip that Kate Moss has left her PR company and will be looking for new representation. Things start to go awry when the plot to approach the model goes horribly wrong and Patsy and Edina find themselves vilified by the entire nation and have to flee the country. Comedy based on the BBC sitcom, starring Jennifer Saunders, Joanna Lumley and Julia Sawalha

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani is taken on a tour of one of London's cemeteries, as she discovers a trove of treasures right on her doorstep and sets out to crack the mystery of a non-native bird. Adam Henson shows how a super-meal is specially concocted for his animals during the winter months, and Tom Heap investigates the controversial decision that allows UK farmers to use a banned pesticide on their fields

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: Mary Beard talks to cultural creatives who make other worlds for people's exploration and delight. Booker Prize-winner Marlon James discusses his Dark Star trilogy, which turns the fantasy genre on its head, and David Greig, artistic director of Edinburgh's Lyceum Theatre, takes viewers on a quest to an ancient woodland, where appealing to the spirits seems completely rational during a pandemic. Mary also chairs a debate on whether the occult is enjoying a revival or if it has always been more popular than she imagines

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: A man is murdered for no apparent reason by a guest at a party. Detectives use CCTV and financial transactions to track the suspect and arrest him just before he flees the country. The forensic team employ a mixture of traditional and digital forensics to try to determine whether or not the witnesses are telling the truth

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste return with more investigations into online dating scams, beginning with the exposure of an elaborate fraud

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

Kate also has some top tips when it comes to jewellery, revealing how to spot an imitation from the real thing.

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: With more than 100 boats in the harbour, Newlyn is Cornwall's biggest fishing port and the outcome of the Brexit trade negotiations could transform the future for its fleet on inshore fishing boats. In the face of an uncertain future, the fleet must prepare for modernisation and whatever challenges or opportunities Brexit may bring

programme.desc: Mark Wright demonstrates exercises designed to make the body strong

programme.title: Politics Live

programme.desc: Tips for studying topics including enzymes and nutrition, poetry and sustainable energy. Plus, more from The Big Read

programme.title: Bitesize: 12-13 Year Olds
programme.desc: Tips for studying topics including photosynthesis, figurative language and sustainable food. Plus, more from The Big Read

programme.title: Bitesize: 13-14 Year Olds
programme.desc: How animals and plants adapt, sonnets and nuclear energy. Plus, more from The Big Read

programme.title: 1066: A Year to Conquer England
programme.desc: As Dan Snow's docudrama continues, King Harold has to take on two invasion forces. First, his brother Tostig attacks the south coast. He is repelled, but there is more to come. Later in the year, a vast Viking invasion force led by King Harald Hardrada of Norway lands in the north of England. Harold rushes to Stamford Bridge to fight for his kingdom and for his life

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Robshaw family enter the turbulent 1910s and the First World War turns all their lives upside down, putting Rochelle back in the kitchen instead of servant Debbie. Never a natural cook, she struggles to feed the family as supplies start to run out and the war puts an end to their previously carefree lives. However, the decade has its positives too with the freedom of a bike ride and an idyllic picnic, and a visit from celebrity chef John Torode - but even he cannot salvage something tasty from 1918's fish sausages

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Four York apartments, including a modern penthouse with a killer view, go head-to-head in order to impress five local judges and be chosen to compete in this week's final

programme.title: The Wonder of Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham explores the capabilities of bears, who can live in practically every habitat on Earth due to being able to endure extreme conditions. The naturalist examines the specialised adaptations that have enabled the mammals to thrive, including how the hollow fur of polar bears allows them to feed during gruelling winters

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and experts David Fletcher and Catherine Southon value antiques at Lulworth Castle in Dorset. Paul also learns how Mary Anning started the craze for fossil-hunting in the early 19th century

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Mina Anwar, Glen Moore, Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Shaun Williamson go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon and the team meet semi-retired couple Becky and David to help them convert their drab old yurt into an a colourful and inviting bijou B&B

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Northern Exposure
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers circumnavigate the Baltic Sea in search of the most exciting food in Europe, beginning with Poland. They create sausages in the countryside with a family who have been making them for generations. The bikers also visit the home of Copernicus, the astronomer who changed the way stargazers look at the universe. There are poignant moments when they visit the Treblinka Extermination Camp, and hailing from ship building communities, Si and Dave are keen to visit the great shipyards of Gdansk to honour the men and women who stood up to the Soviet regime

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: As they fight to secure a place in the semi-finals, the four remaining designers are challenged to transform two hair salons in Richmond, London, and for the first time in the competition, the designers will see their spaces before creating their visions. Presenter Alan Carr and judge Michelle Ogundehin will then decide who makes it through to the next stage

programme.title: DNA Family Secrets
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley helps people answer life-changing questions by examining their DNA. This time, Stacy meets 31-year-old Duri, who fears she may carry a potentially fatal genetic mutation and wants to test her DNA to see if she could suffer from the same disease as her mother. Elsewhere, a woman who was adopted when she was just six-months-old wants to use DNA technology to finally uncover information about her ancestry

programme.title: Funny Festival Live
programme.desc: Stand-up performances by established and up-and-coming comedians, recorded in front of a live virtual audience over three nights at a Covid-safe venue in south London

programme.title: Harry Hill's Lonely Island
programme.desc: Silent slapstick comedy short from Harry Hill

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Alan Partridge: Alpha Papa (2013)
programme.desc: The hapless local radio DJ ends up in the centre of a crisis when a colleague gets fired by the station's new owners, only to respond by returning to the studio with a shotgun and taking hostages. The police enlist Alan's aid as a negotiator, giving him the chance to revive his own flagging public profile. Comedy, starring Steve Coogan, Colm Meaney and Felicity Montagu

programme.title: Your Garden Made Perfect
programme.desc: Suzanne and Andrew have a huge problem in their lives - a giant derelict swimming pool in their back garden in Plymouth. A succession of landscaping companies have run a mile the moment they gaze into the abyss, and their budget does not even stretch to simply filling it in - so how will designers Tom Massey and Pip Probert transform this unique outdoor space? Meanwhile, Joel Bird comes to the help of an Essex couple with a nightmare patio. Presented by Angela Scanlon

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti work in Ashford Castle, Co Mayo. Set in 350 acres, it was founded 800 years ago by the Anglo-Norman De Burgo family, and much later became the family home of the Guinness family. With the help of hotel historian, Fintan, Giles discovers that the castle has had a rollercoaster journey through the centuries, sporadically reaching moments of real jeopardy, only to be saved by a turn of events or new owners. Giles and Monica soon learn that the secret of the castle's success is the people who work here and the hotel's strong, long-running community ties

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon and the team meet semi-retired couple Becky and David to help them convert their drab old yurt into an a colourful and inviting bijou B&B

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles presents the show from Wrexham, North Wales, with experts Caroline Hawley and Danny Sebastian, and heads to Chester Cathedral to learn how spiders inspired artists

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Master craftsman Simon Parfett and his team help people with a little land or a beautiful garden generate extra income by creating their own unique B&Bs. They begin by heading to the picturesque Wye Valley to help George and Al transform their battered old family camper van

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon Parfett and the team are in Herefordshire to meet new parents Cheryl and Bill and help get their chalet on a hill from flat-pack to Scandinavian chic

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: Mark Wright's daily dose of workout sessions. Today, fitness routines to get the heart pumping, joined by Strictly Come Dancing professional Janette Manrara

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Bitesize: 11-12 Year Olds
programme.desc: Topics include pure and impure substances, and studying in Spanish

programme.title: Bitesize: 12-13 Year Olds
programme.desc: Chemical reactions and how rates of pay are calculated

programme.title: Bitesize: 13-14 Year Olds
programme.desc: Topics include the structure of the Earth and calculating density

programme.title: 1066: A Year to Conquer England
programme.desc: After months trapped in a port in northern France, the winds finally change and Duke William leads his vast invasion fleet across the Channel. Catastrophically, King Harold is 300 miles away in the north, having defeated the Vikings. Harold rushes back south, gathers a new army, and heads to Hastings to take on this new invasion. The two armies will meet at Battle in Sussex on October 14, 1066, and the outcome will change the shape of England and Europe for ever. Dan Snow presents

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Robshaw family enter the 1920s, sampling their first taste of the racy reputation of the decade as they host a jazz-age party fuelled by a menu of 14 different cocktails. The period has something new for everyone, with sweet treats for Fred, spicy food for Brandon in a 1920s curry house and the kicks and tricks of the Charleston for Miranda and Ros with former Strictly Come Dancing stars James and Ola Jordan

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three semi-detached homes in York - including a two-up, two-down 1930s house reinvented for the modern market and a 1970s communal building - are in the spotlight

programme.title: The Wonder of Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham examines new scientific research that shows how subtle adaptations in the anatomy and physiology of big cats can contribute to the success of all stages of a hunt. He looks at how leg hairs help leopards stalk their prey, and how an enlarged area of lionesses' brains give them the edge

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques experts Mark Stacey and Michael Baggott appraise an array of collectibles and antiques at Southwell Minster, Nottinghamshire, including a classic Rolling Stones LP and a haul of Russian silver. Presenter Paul Martin also finds time to explore the county's Newstead Abbey, once home to 19th-century poet Lord Byron

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Mina Anwar, Glen Moore, Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Shaun Williamson go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Master craftsman Simon Parfett and his team transform an attic in the oldest house in the village of Wrington, North Somerset into a writer's retreat-themed B&B

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Northern Exposure
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King travel on to the Baltic States, starting with Lithuania. They make a pilgrimage to the Hill of Crosses, a site historically under constant threat from the KGB. In Latvia the travelling cooks ride with the region's oldest biker gang, The Brothers of the Wind. Estonia sees the duo going back to basics on the island of Muhu. They get a taste of the future with an inventive chef, go foraging for sea kale and fall in love with a group of dancing grannies

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a bakery that produces 180,000 Cornish pasties a day, and discovers the very specific rule one of the pastries must comply with to genuinely be considered Cornish. Ruth Goodman debunks some myths about pasties and investigates the history of the pepper trade, while Cherry Healey learns about the science of onions - including why cutting them can bring tears to the eyes

programme.title: The Terror
programme.desc: Crozier demands that Franklin send out a sledge party to seek help, but he is reluctant to risk any more of his men. The resulting clash leads Franklin to condemn Crozier for his alcoholism, prompting his devastated old friend to take matters into his own hands, drafting a resignation letter and taking personal responsibility for finding help

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: Comedy, written, directed by and starring Diane Morgan. Mandy Carter is a woman with big dreams - and today her dream is to find work. But the only thing available to her is a job in a fruit packing factory, where a big mallet will come in handy. With Michelle Greenidge and Tom Basden

programme.title: Funny Festival Live
programme.desc: Stand-up performances by established and up-and-coming comedians, recorded in front of a live virtual audience over three nights at a Covid-safe venue in south London

programme.title: Harry Hill's Lonely Island
programme.desc: Silent slapstick comedy short with Harry Hill appearing as a monkey in a tree trying to grab an apple

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: David Brent: Life on the Road (2016)
programme.desc: More than a decade on from his appearance in documentary series The Office, a film crew is once again following the life of David Brent. He is now working as a rep for a cleaning-goods distributor, but harbours dreams of making it big in the music business. Acting on this, he takes three weeks off work and tours with a new-look version of Foregone Conclusion, the band he fronted in the 1990s. Comedy, directed by and starring Ricky Gervais

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: The six remaining designers are put into teams of two and challenged to transform three shops - a pet accessories shop, a camera store, and a kitchen equipment retailer. Each designer must make a pitch to the business owner they have been assigned to, meaning that three contenders will get their idea approved, while the others act as their assistants. Helping Michelle Ogundehin decide who should stay or go this week is guest judge and pop-up retail guru Ross Bailey

programme.title: Trump Takes On the World
programme.desc: After one of the most shocking presidencies in history, Donald Trump's top advisers and the leaders who clashed with him lift the lid on the critical moments of his foreign policy. In the third and final programme, Trump sets his sights on Asia, and determined to go ahead with his new tough approach, he launches the biggest trade war in economic history. Trump's unorthodox style is on full display as he switches between maximum pressure and maximum flattery, as he forms the most unlikely of friendships with one of the world's most brutal dictators, Kim Jong-un

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste investigate the case of a man who thought he had found love on a dating site with a woman who began making demands for money

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman hosts the semi-final of the cooking challenge. Mary Berry challenges the remaining contestants to prepare a fish supper, Chris Bavin asks them to come up with a meal that puts mushrooms front and centre, and Angela Hartnett sets them the task of making crab ravioli with homemade pasta dough. After a dramatic end to the final challenge, one more celebrity leaves the kitchen

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's World of Steam, Steel and Stone
programme.desc: The steeplejack's fascination with mining, which led him to dig his own coal mine in his back garden. Fred was also keen to teach viewers about the history of the industry, educating people about the dangers faced by those working down the pit, and the skills workers required

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: A cabin finds a peaceful resting place in a warm setting of lush, lakeside greenery in this oval-shaped painting by Bob Ross

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and art expert Philip Mould travel to Australia to verify whether a painting bought from an English auction site is a lost work by impressionist Tom Roberts, one of the country's most important artists. The owners have fallen on hard times, and desperately need to prove the painting is genuine. The investigation leads them to retrace the time Roberts spent in Britain and uncovers some alarming forgeries

programme.title: The Story of Welsh Art
programme.desc: Huw Stephens explores how, from the 18th-century, the dramatic landscape of Wales captivated artists like Richard Wilson and JMW Turner, who painted it in ways that changed the course of art history. Huw also discovers the work of Penry Williams, a Merthyr Tydfil artist who was commissioned to paint his home town's vast Cyfarthfa ironworks

programme.title: Women in Film: BBC Introducing Arts
programme.desc: Cultural historian Janina Ramirez presents a collection of intriguing and exciting short films by emerging women directors and artists. Each film gives a female perspective on modern day topics from body image and new love to grief and belonging

programme.title: Life Cinematic
programme.desc: The Bafta-winning writer-director, whose movies include Belle and A United Kingdom, discusses the films that have influenced her career. Presented by Edith Bowman

programme.title: Arena
programme.desc: This profile, featuring an audio interview with Cindy Sherman, gives a rare insight into one of the most elusive and influential living artists. Contributors include Kim Cattrall and Gaby Hoffmann

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: A cabin finds a peaceful resting place in a warm setting of lush, lakeside greenery in this oval-shaped painting by Bob Ross

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's World of Steam, Steel and Stone

Huw also discovers the work of Penry Williams, a Merthyr Tydfil artist who was commissioned to paint his home town's vast Cyfarthfa ironworks

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's World of Steam, Steel and Stone
programme.desc: The steeplejack's interest in industrial engineering, which was sparked by the building of canals, railways and bridges near his Bolton home. The documentary visits Hadrian's Wall, the canal tunnels in Dudley and Conway's suspension bridge

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a serene lakeside setting accented with an abundance of his trademark trees

programme.title: Yes Minister
programme.desc: Cutbacks to the civil service threaten the permanent secretary's position so Sir Humphrey tries to manipulate Hacker into abandoning his new policy. Satirical comedy, starring Paul Eddington and Nigel Hawthorne

programme.title: The Fall and Rise of Reginald Perrin
programme.desc: Reggie decides to have an affair and invites secretary Joan home for a spot of unauthorised overtime - but finds his relatives thwart his attempts at seduction. Leonard Rossiter and Sue Nicholls star

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: The world's leading experts discuss where they would go on Mars, if they got the chance, and what they would need to survive on the planet. In the ultimate traveller's guide to Mars and using real images and data, Horizon brings Martian landmarks to life, from vast plains and towering volcanoes to deep valleys and hidden underground caverns, and also show where to land, where to live and even where to hunt for traces of extraterrestrial life. Narrated by Mark Gatiss

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: An edition first shown in July 2012 focusing on Curiosity, Nasa's mission to investigate the possibility of life on Mars. The agency hoped to land a nuclear-powered rover vehicle, which cost $2.5billion to develop, on the surface of the planet by winching it down from a rocket-powered crane, then use it to explore the terrain. The programme features contributions by the project's chief scientist John Grotzinger and discovers the lessons experts learned from previous expeditions, many of which ended in failure

programme.title: Britain and the Sea
programme.desc: David Dimbleby sails along the coast of East Anglia, exploring how Britain's view of the sea has changed over the past 200 years. The coast and the beach became a playground for the nation, inspiring artists, transforming coastal architecture and creating a seaside culture that remains uniquely British. Setting out from Gorleston-on-Sea, he travels down the Suffolk and Essex coasts and into the Thames, until he reaches Greenwich. Along the way he discovers works of art that reflect Britain's evolving relationship with the sea, meets a sand sculptor and visits the Queen's House in Greenwich, built in 1616 as a waterside retreat for Queen Anne and now home to Britain's greatest collection of maritime artefacts

programme.title: Man in Room 301
programme.desc: Seppo travels to Greece, where Risto questions Niina about how well she knows Leo. In Finnish

programme.title: Man in Room 301
programme.desc: Olivia is frantic with worry when neither Anna nor Tytti return home from Sonia's party. In Greece, Kalle sneaks out of the hotel room to go climbing with Leo. In Finnish

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a serene lakeside setting accented with an abundance of his trademark trees

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's World of Steam, Steel and Stone
programme.desc: The steeplejack's interest in industrial engineering, which was sparked by the building of canals, railways and bridges near his Bolton home. The documentary visits Hadrian's Wall, the canal tunnels in Dudley and Conway's suspension bridge

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: The world's leading experts discuss where they would go on Mars, if they got the chance, and what they would need to survive on the planet. In the ultimate traveller's guide to Mars and using real images and data, Horizon brings Martian landmarks to life, from vast plains and towering volcanoes to deep valleys and hidden underground caverns, and also show where to land, where to live and even where to hunt for traces of extraterrestrial life. Narrated by Mark Gatiss

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's World of Steam, Steel and Stone
programme.desc: The steeplejack shares his appreciation for the skills of the men who built Britain's great castles, palaces and country houses. He draws on his experiences as an apprentice joiner to offer demonstrations of building techniques, including the humorous falling-down arch

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross's exciting use of blue, yellow and orange hues brings vibrancy to a cabin in a woodside setting beside a clear blue lake

programme.title: Armada - 12 Days to Save England
programme.desc: Dan Snow relives the events of the naval battle in 1588, drawing on recently rediscovered documents to get a sense of the key events in the conflict and exposing the tensions among the Spanish commanders. Dramatic reconstructions go behind the scenes in Elizabeth I's royal court, revealing the impact the prospect of invasion had on the queen and her advisors as she tried to maintain a strong public image while under the threat of an assassination attempt. With analysis from Professor Saul David and Dr Sam Willis

programme.title: Elizabeth R
programme.desc: Award-winning drama chronicling the reign of Elizabeth I. Mary Queen of Scots is imprisoned at Chartley, while Elizabeth ponders her fate. Starring Glenda Jackson, Vivian Pickles, David Collings and Stephen Murray

programme.title: Queen Elizabeth's Secret Agents
programme.desc: Documentary featuring dramatic reconstructions and interviews with historians to examine the network of spymasters and secret agents that helped protect Elizabeth I from assassination, terror and treason for more than 40 years. The first edition reveals how the pope's declaration of Elizabeth as a heretic 12 years into her reign set England's Catholics against her, leading the queen to charge William Cecil with the task of stopping assassins getting through, and the huge espionage network he established saw his spies break Catholic conspiracies at home and abroad

programme.title: The Story of Welsh Art
programme.desc: Huw Stephens explores how, from the 18th century, the dramatic landscape of Wales captivated artists like Richard Wilson and JMW Turner, who painted it in ways that changed the course of art history. Huw also discovers the work of Penry Williams, a Merthyr Tydfil artist who was commissioned to paint his home town's vast Cyfarthfa ironworks

Dramatic reconstructions go behind the scenes in Elizabeth I's royal court, revealing the impact the prospect of invasion had on the queen and her advisors as she tried to maintain a strong public image while under the threat of an assassination attempt. With analysis from Professor Saul David and Dr Sam Willis

programme.title: Little Big Awesome
programme.desc: While Gluko preps for his pickling license exam, Lennon struggles with a changing friend.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: The crimebusting rodent faces his toughest challenge ever as a mysterious super-villain releases all the villains from Arkwright

programme.title: Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed!
programme.desc: It’s Pieface’s turn to look after class hamster, Space Captain Harrison McScruff-Butt. His own self doubt and Walter’s resolve to sabotage him lead to a rescue mission to free Scruff-Butt from Walter’s mansion.

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: Lila has a brand new vlog that goes viral in school. Girl doesn’t like seeing her nasty rival have such success and so begins filming her own vlog about her family’s real life.

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Topical news magazine for children.

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: Youth activist Anita takes us on a journey to show the impact fast fashion has on our world. She is determined that fashion can be sustainable. Our ‘buy and throw away’ culture contributes to the global warming crisis so something has to be done. In a vivid animated world, we learn about the journey that many of our clothes make; from where they are manufactured, all the way to how they are transported to the UK high streets - but are there other alternatives when buying our clothes? Anita sets out to explore sustainable solutions for young people. She talks to environmentalist Zaqiya about her school initiative, Swop it Up, and Holly, manager of the UK’s largest charity shop. Together they show us ways young people can buy fashionable clothes and look after the planet at the same time.

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: Specialist agents investigate all sorts of strange and weird happenings in their town by calling on their skills in mathematics and numeracy

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: Coding Genius follows 11-tear-old Emmanuella, who has a passion for coding. She hit the headlines in the UK after setting up lessons for school kids like her and is determined to go further. Now based in the Nigerian capital Abuja, Emmanuella has work to do - she wants to pass on her love of coding to other girls in the city and launches CodeKid, an after-school, weekend and holiday club for girls aged 6 – 15. But will they turn up, or will the thought of coding keep them away? In this short film, we find out what started Emmanuella’s love of coding, and get to know her extraordinary ambitions for changing the world, one coding club at a time.

programme.title: Danny & Mick
programme.desc: Danny is convinced it's Joy's birthday and he sets about organising a surprise party

programme.title: Art Ninja
programme.desc: Ricky throws Rich a surprise fancy dress birthday party but disaster strikes when everyone turns up dressed as clowns - his worst nightmare! Ricky gets inspired to make art with a real ‘wow' factor and things get messy when people start throwing custard pies!

programme.title: Our CBBC
programme.desc: Brothers Max and Harry play golfball, a sport for visually impaired and blind people

programme.title: Bitesize: 5-7 Year Olds
programme.desc: Manchester United's Jesse Lingard reads Attack of the Demon Dinner Ladies in Book Club.

programme.title: Bitesize: 7-9 Year Olds
programme.desc: Joanna Lumley reads You're a Bad Man Mr Gum in Book Club.

programme.title: Bitesize: 9-11 Year Olds
programme.desc: WWE wrestler Becky Lynch reads A Pocket Full of Stars in Book Club.

programme.title: Celebrity Supply Teacher
programme.desc: American actor and voice artist Jacob Hopkins gives a lesson all the way from Los Angeles on the art of voice acting. He demonstrates exercises that he uses to strengthen the voice and how to use it for greater effect. He shows how to warm up the voice, how to build a character through the use of voice techniques and illustrates the differences between on-screen and voice acting.

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: Kathryn Drysdale stars in an episode focusing on the life of the Egyptian queen, meeting her as she clashes with her brother, seizes power and hooks up with Roman leaders Julius Caesar and Mark Antony. Plus, the thoughts of famous Chinese philosopher Confucius, and the less glamorous side of life in Rome

programme.title: Our School
programme.desc: How would you cope with a whole day of lessons while sitting in a bath of gunge?

programme.title: Ricky Wilson's Art Jam
programme.desc: TV presenter and DJ Kem Cetinay visits the Art Jam studio to show Ricky three items that mean the world to him - his first pair of hairdressing scissors, his dog Rocky’s collar and his grandfather’s shepherd whistle. While chatting to Ricky, Kem shares amazing stories about how learning to be a hairdresser boosted his confidence, how his dog Rocky has helped improve his mental health, his incredible family history and the fun he has as a TV presenter.

programme.title: Out of This World
programme.desc: Dr Maggie uncovers the people and the missions that are tasked with looking for aliens in space and asks whether life really is out there and how will we find out. Dr Maggie’s star guest Sofia Sheikh is searching for intelligent life in the universe, while Maggie answers all your space questions. Her Top 5 is of the places where we might find extraterrestrial life. Buckle up, it’s time to go Out Of This World!

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: Dr Chris puts Dr Xand inside a giant water balloon to show how viruses attack our bodies

programme.title: The Deep
programme.desc: Fontaine believes a fish is responsible for wrecking Dolos's boat, sinking the Aronnax, and almost destroying the Rover, so she tries to get rid of it and its bad luck

programme.title: The Deep
programme.desc: The Rover is swallowed by a supposedly extinct creature, and as Will and Fontaine cling on just inside its mouth, Kaiko and Ant face a desperate race against time to free them

programme.title: Mighty Mustangs
programme.desc: Marnie cannot wait to get started at the start of the new football season. The only problem is, there is no coach, no uniforms and no other players

programme.title: Mighty Mustangs
programme.desc: When the Mustangs get accused of 'playing like girls', it's the last straw for Marnie. It's time to prove themselves by beating the boys. So they challenge them to a golden goal game

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: Historical characters and events are explored in sketches and quizzes based on the books by Terry Deary. Comedy talent including Sarah Hadland, Steve Punt and Meera Syal help bring the strange facts, gory battles and crazy inventions to life

programme.title: The Dumping Ground
programme.desc: When the office window gets smashed, Bird quickly finds himself as the main suspect. Desperate to prove his innocence, Bird searches for the true culprit behind the crime.

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: Lila has a brand new vlog that goes viral in school. Girl doesn’t like seeing her nasty rival have such success and so begins filming her own vlog about her family’s real life.

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: Lydia's hair started to fall out in small patches at five years old, and she was diagnosed with the autoimmune condition alopecia areata. After years of worrying, attending various hospital appointments and using medicines that didn't improve condition significantly, she decided to shave her hair off at the age of tejn. Now she embraces her beautiful bald look, but sometimes chooses to wear a wig. With the support of her twin brother and family we take a look into her life. Lydia is determined to become a paramedic, spread awareness about alopecia and help others to love themselves, however they look.

programme.title: We Bare Bears
programme.desc: After being rescued at a farmer's market, Panda believes he has found the woman of his dreams

programme.title: She-Ra
programme.desc: Glimmer prepares to take on the role of Queen of Bright Moon. Meanwhile, Castaspella plans the elaborate coronation, aided by Adora, Bow and the Princess Alliance.

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: Fourteen-year-olds Kyra and Sophie started rescuing hedgehogs in their Stratford-Upon-Avon home when they were just nine years old. They have now saved over 600 hedgehogs, rescuing, rehabilitating and then releasing them back into the wild. We meet Kyra and Sophie in their garden hospital, and follow the rescue, medical care, rehabilitation and release of some of the poorly hedgehogs that they have been looking after.

programme.title: Odd Squad

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: Documentary following 14-year-old cheerleader and dancer Neisha as she works with disabled choreographer Kate Stanforth to devise an artistic dance video with a one-tonne industrial robot! The film combines a compelling human-interest story about how Neisha’s disability has become her strength, with first-class choreography. Neisha’s dance is one of triumph and tenderness, inspiring others to find their own strength despite the obstacles that may lie in their way.

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: Specialist agents investigate all sorts of strange and weird happenings in their town by calling on their skills in mathematics and numeracy

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: Fourteen-year-old Mae is fed up with people misunderstanding her autism and has created her own guide to try and help people understand. She teaches us that everyone with autism is different and not to make assumptions. She also shows that she can be affectionate, likes being who she is, and that it's ok to be different!

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: Chelcea’s biggest passion is for hair! In this documentary, she shows us how she does her hair at home, doing her own hair, her mum’s hair, her sister’s hair, her friend’s hair and even her dad’s beard. Shemhas been doing lots of different hairstyles for everyone, inlcuding twists, braids (thick and small), plaits, tops buns, and also leaving it out as an afro, which is really good for the hair and scalp. Chelcea is an expert on the science of hair and how to look after it, and is particularity interested in natural hair remedies. Chelcea’s parents encourage their daughter’s passion and, through beautiful animation, we learn about her mum's childhood in the Democratic Republic of Congo. By taking us on her own hair journey, Chelcea’s determination to inspire others to celebrate their amazing hair shines through!

programme.desc: The Twirlywoos are beside a river. They watch a fisherman make his rod longer and longer and longer. The fisherman casts out the line and then settles down for a nap. The fisherman wakes to discover a long, long, long fish at the end of his line. The Twirlywoo Screen draws a small line that Chickedy and Chick help make longer. Toodloo helps the line get a little longer still but when Great BigHoo taps the screen, the line becomes so long it shoots off the Twirlywoo Screen. Meanwhile, Myles and his dad heads to a local park, where they look at the lake and see lots of different birds. Iffah’s family go to an indoor play area, where Iffah has fun playing in the ball pool. programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe.
