programme.desc: Join Phinehas and Charlie as they both wait for their newborn baby brother or sister to arrive into the world. Charlie’s mum packs a bag ready to go to hospital with dad and waves goodbye to Charlie and Grandma, whilst Phinehas’ family gets ready to pop a gender reveal balloon to find out if the next arrival will be a boy or a girl. Before the big moment, Charlie wraps up a cuddly elephant toy with his Grandma as a gift for his new brother or sister. After a long wait, it’s finally time for Charlie and Phinehas to meet their new siblings. Both babies travel home to their families and meet their big brothers for the very first time. Once Phinehas meets the new baby, he joins in with dad to register the birth of their new baby. Charlie meets a health visitor, who has come to visit to check on the baby and make sure it is staying healthy and strong.

programme.title: What's On Your Head?
programme.desc: A closer look at scuba diving gear. Mark tells us all about how his Scuba diving mask allows him to come face to face with amazing creatures under the sea, like jellyfish, dolphins and even sharks!

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: Giant rocks appear all over the forest, so the Olobobs make Swifty Lifty who takes Gurdy to have a look

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Scottish lad Connor demonstrates how to make delicious vegetarian haggis, served with freshly mashed 'neeps and tatties'

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle makes two new doggy friends leaving Evie feeling a bit left out until Waffle reassures her. The two new dogs escape into Mrs Hobbs' garden, so Waffle has to save the day

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: The lamb decides to have a day collecting blue toys, cushions and paint, but finds he needs more than one colour to create a picture

programme.title: Tinga Tinga Tales
programme.desc: African folk tale, telling the story of why fleas jump

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say ‘scooter’, and we go on a whirlwind word adventure! We see lots of different scooters – a purple scooter, a blue scooter and a bopping scooter. There’s a new scooter, a toot your hooter scooter and a family on scooters. We even see Dee and Monkey enjoy a ride on racing scooters. The journey is repeated with some special surprises along the way, and everyone has a boogie when Dee’s friend finally succeeds in saying today’s word!

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: Introducing the letter M as the ever-munching M and friends make a gingerbread man and chase after him on a flying mat - using a handy map to keep them hot on his trail

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Ten presents a rundown of the top hit songs in Numberland. Learn number bonds to six, seven, eight, nine and ten with the Numberblocks.

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Dog and Pug go an adventure underground where they meet Granny Pug. They slide down a tunnel to the centre of the earth. When they arrive, they find some very friendly dinosaurs. Granny Pug promises to keep the dinosaur’s home a secret.

programme.title: Messy Goes to OKIDO
programme.desc: Messy has to save Okido from a falling comet before his bedtime! He sees that Mayor Oki still has his bath!

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friends go on a treasure hunt, and Mr Tumble's cake goes on an adventure!

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Fun and games for youngsters with the playful quartet

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: It is a windy day and Bing wants to fly his kite with Flop, but insists on doing it without help. When the toy gets caught in a park bench, the rabbit needs an extra pair of hands

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: The animated adventures of an energetic baby monkey and his long-suffering mother, who take a light-hearted approach to problem-solving and teamwork as they tackle life's challenges together

programme.title: Ferne and Rory's Vet Tales
programme.desc: Rory visits Porter and his pet bearded dragon, Beardy, who has a poorly tummy and needs a trip to the vet. Ferne joins a mobile vet on the Isle of Wight, where they visit lots of pets who need care

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Brothers Bobby and Lenny make a volcano from paper and glue

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: Grace shows off three amazing machines, all of which are different kinds of motorbikes

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Captain Captain insists the pirates be more sensible

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee introduces the squirrels to Bucky, his old rocking horse - but first it needs to be repaired

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Cotton-tail's birthday cake is stolen, but when Peter tries to get it back, he runs into a host of angry villains

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: While on a treasure hunt on Faraway Island, Bitz and Bob have to rescue Purl from the clutches of Pirate Bevelbeard and his ship mates

programme.title: What's On Your Head?
programme.desc: Jamie Chadwick, one of the world’s best female racing drivers, tells us how her helmet helps her to drive faster than everyone else.

programme.title: Ferne & Rory's Teeny Tiny Creatures
programme.desc: Holly wants to know all there is to know about wriggly earthworms! Ferne visits a worm farm and meets worm expert Ken, and they handle different species of UK worms. Meanwhile, Rory and his son Sandy search the compost bin and the garden for worms and decide to build a wormery to make worm tea! Plus, we find out why earthworms are so important to the planet by being amazing soil engineers.

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. He works there with his boss, Mr Hammond and tech wizard Jen, who monitors all the animals from the Aqua Base. Jen has just got back from a bird-spotting trip in the Galapagos Islands, and Andy is keen to learn how it went. Jen tells Andy that thanks to the pocket bird book Mr Hammond lent her she was able to spot lots of sea birds she had never seen before. Andy is interested in the book and asks to see it, but unfortunately on the last day of Jen’s trip a waved albatross swopped down and carried it away. Mr Hammond calls from his new office in the Galapagos dome and is wearing ear protectors due to a peculiar loud noise which he’d like them to investigate. He also asks Jen for his bird book so that he can do some bird spotting from his new office. Andy informs Mr Hammond that Jen is still unpacking so will bring the bird book over shortly. It’s time to go on an aquatic

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Grandmaster Glitch makes the Statue of Liberty fall, right into the path of a passing ferry

programme.title: GiggleQuiz
programme.desc: Dina Lady and The Lost Pirate join team captains Gail Force and Keith Fitt for funny clips and question

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Animated fun for younger viewers featuring tales of the world's worst puppy who woofs and wags his way into the lives of an unassuming family

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo and Gran Gran head to the library in search of the new Captain Chloe book, but they discover the children's section is closed for redecoration. JoJo, Gran Gran and their friends all help to paint a pirate picture on the library wall.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Scarf Lady's old film reels get themselves in a tangle, so Sarah and Duck decide to recreate the footage. Animated adventures of a seven-year-old girl and her duck

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: Inside the little Toy House, everybody is sharing a book with pictures of tidy things

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Major invents something new, and Tiny and Small give him an idea

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Makka Pakka loses his sponge while cleaning stones

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Emily Watson tells viewers it is important to find your own way to say how you feel, in this story about a tapir who wants to write, but is lost for words

programme.desc: Sonali Shah showcases a selection of Cotswolds properties to a couple from Essex who are hoping their budget of £550,000 will buy them a new home with three bedrooms, one of which could be used for a home office. The presenter also visits a quarry from which Cotswold stone is used for new builds and conservation projects

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Mother and daughter Jo and Millie from Berwick-upon-Tweed run a food waste cafe. Today they are looking for new ways to use the food that is most often donated. Ellis Barrie and Romy Gill come up with a couple of ideas, while Rylan Clark-Neal keeps things ticking over

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ash and Peter agree to try again after their disastrous date, sharing a passionate kiss just as Iqra arrives. Lucas overhears Chelsea arguing with Jack and accuses his daughter of bullying but she reminds him that if the job goes wrong, it is her life on the line. Later, Lucas makes a date with Karen - but doesn't give her his real name

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Chloe is suspended, unable to explain why one of her patients died - and as she leaves she confides her suspicion to Ange that Cameron was involved in the patient's death. Sahira considers bumping her father up the transplant list and on Lucky's last day she pulls out all the stops to help a patient with a phobia of hospitals. Will it be enough for Max to keep the psychiatric nurse on at Holby?

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Previously unseen material from the comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: Caroline Aherne at the BBC
programme.desc: A celebration of comedy writer and actress Caroline Aherne, featuring highlights of her work for the BBC including The Mrs Merton Show and The Royle Family. Presented by Fast Show colleague John Thomson

programme.title: The Royle Family
programme.desc: The morning of the wedding arrives and it is not just Denise's nerves that are on edge. Jim has diarrhoea and a constipated Nana warns the Royle bride that it's all downhill from here

programme.title: BBC Wales Live
programme.desc: Bethan Rhys Roberts presents the weekly show featuring stories and in-depth interviews about what is happening in Wales and why it matters

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Having spent the night with Bradley, a contented Norman returns home to find that his mother is in jail, arrested for the murder of Keith Summers. Her bail is set at $100,000, but she refuses her sons' offer to help her. Norman puts up the hotel as collateral for a bond, but Norma tries to make him feel guilty about his not being there when she was arrested

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Dylan tells Gil what happened to Ethan and gets a new partner - Remo - while Norma has a hard time accepting that Shelby kept Jiao a prisoner in his basement. Emma wants to call the FBI immediately to tell them about the sex ring, but Norma wants to get Keith Summers' belt from Shelby before they do anything

programme.title: Moving On
programme.desc: A divorcee finds a new lease of life when his daughter buys him a course of fitness sessions for his 65th birthday. However, his new hobby threatens to drive a wedge between him and his close-knit group of friends. Starring Les Dennis and Liz McClarnon, with Aaron Cobham, Louis Emerick and Michael Starke

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A couple with a budget of £600,000 search for a rural home in the Lancashire countryside, and later Ginny Buckley visits the Victorian seaside resort of Blackpool

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Birmingham mother and son Julie and Rich take to the kitchen - she to push the boundaries and discover new flavours, he to learn new ideas to impress his vegan girlfriend

programme.title: The Bidding Room

programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Political Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Welsh Liberal Democrats

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Pizza Boys
programme.desc: Jez and Ieuz's trip leads them to Naples, the spiritual home of pizza. It's a matter of days before the competition, so they need to focus on tracking down the best ingredients for their pizza. They're also keen to pick up any last minute tips, so they arrange a series of masterclasses with some of the most renowned pizzaiolos in the city

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metal maestro Dominic Chinea gets to work on a crumbling cast-iron garden gate, whose design includes the badge of the Royal Lancers, while horologist Steve fletcher and leather expert Suzie Fletcher join up to restore a wristwatch awarded for bravery in the First World War. Upholsterer Sonnaz Nooranvary takes on a rocking chair that has been at the heart of a family home for decades, and Geoff Harvey is given a 1920s penny arcade game

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Bank Balance
programme.desc: Gordon welcomes two more pairs - partners Caroline and Andrew, and brothers Sam and Sassan. Will either duo have what it takes to beat the board and walk away with the jackpot of £100,000?

programme.title: I Can't Say My Name: Stammering in the Spotlight
programme.desc: BBC producer Felicity Baker has a stammer that she has spent her whole life trying to hide. She struggles to say her own name. Now she is stepping out in front of the camera for the first time to talk about what it is like to live with the condition - and she soon finds she is not alone. She meets a rugby player who blames his stammer for his aggressive behaviour on the pitch, a rapper who found music stopped him stammering and the comedy star Michael Palin, whose father stammered but never ever spoke about it

programme.title: Zara McDermott: Revenge Porn
programme.desc: Zara McDermott shot to fame on Love Island in 2018. But as soon as she left the villa, Zara was told that naked photos she had shared with an ex-lover had started circulating worldwide. The incident left Zara devastated, ashamed and looking for answers. Here she sets out to understand more about the impact of revenge porn on victims and questions whether anyone can safely share intimate images again

programme.title: Catch Her If You Can
programme.desc: Documentary following an attempt to expose a successful London businesswoman's double life as a con artist. A suspicious invoice tipped off a potential victim, and inspired her to track down other parties who had been fleeced in the past. Together, they came up with a plan to expose the woman's misdeeds

programme.desc: While Alex suffers the consequence of Tyler's ire, Amber finally sees the impact of her actions and Lenny discovers her twisted plan for the Murdoch family. Angus tries to smooth things over with Rebecca after their disastrous date night, and Caitlin supports an isolated Eve

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Chloe is suspended, unable to explain why one of her patients died - and as she leaves she confides her suspicion to Ange that Cameron was involved in the patient's death. Sahira considers bumping her father up the transplant list and on Lucky's last day she pulls out all the stops to help a patient with a phobia of hospitals. Will it be enough for Max to keep the psychiatric nurse on at Holby?

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: Paul Murton travels through old Perthshire. He lends a hand bringing in the cattle and hears how Rob Roy MacGregor might not have been the romantic figure of popular fiction. He also travels along a disused railway to a place where the earth once moved for folk at the 'Shaky Toun', and enjoys a trek on horseback to Loch Tay, where he goes back to the iron age and learns how to light a fire without matches

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metal maestro Dominic Chinea gets to work on a crumbling cast-iron garden gate, whose design includes the badge of the Royal Lancers, while horologist Steve fletcher and leather expert Suzie Fletcher join up to restore a wristwatch awarded for bravery in the First World War. programme.desc: Gordon welcomes two more pairs - partners Caroline and Andrew, and brothers Sam and Sassan. Will either duo have what it takes to beat the board and walk away with the jackpot of £100,000?

programme.title: Zara McDermott: Revenge Porn
programme.desc: Zara McDermott shot to fame on Love Island in 2018. But as soon as she left the villa, Zara was told that naked photos she had shared with an ex-lover had started circulating worldwide. The incident left Zara devastated, ashamed and looking for answers. Here she sets out to understand more about the impact of revenge porn on victims and questions whether anyone can safely share intimate images again

programme.title: Scottish Questions
programme.desc: Ministers are put on the spot by fellow MPs in their monthly question-and-answer session at Westminster

programme.title: The Edit

programme.title: Debate Night
programme.desc: Topical debate on the big issues affecting Scotland and beyond. Members of the public have an hour to put their questions to a panel of politicians and other public figures about the biggest stories and the things they care about the most

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym and Gethin are joined by Dr Xand, who is on hand with his medical advice and support. Naga Munchetty talks about her new Panorama investigation into racism, and we launch a brand new dance series for Red Nose Day

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin explore properties about to be auctioned in Upstreet in Kent, Yardley in Birmingham, and Constantine in Cornwall

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel tries to stop a dangerous hunger strike, Ruhma offers an ear as pressure builds on Luca, and Zara worries for a grieving friend who believes in communing with the dead

programme.title: Moving On
programme.desc: Five dramas linked by the central theme of moving on. A man is stunned when his father, whom he thought was dead, turns up at his wedding

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a young canine-loving couple from Hertfordshire, who are hoping for a fresh start in a new High Weald home, and have a budget of £600,000

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Akis Petretzikis and Anna Haugh prepare meals in 20 minutes, using ingredients chosen by two best friends, one of whom likes traditional cooking while the other is more adventurous

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers are intrigued by a vintage petrol lighter based on Donald Campbell's land speed record racing car, a Georgian needlework box, a 1970s kitsch musical drinks decanter and a collection of Second World War memorabilia. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Panorama

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Denise confronts Chelsea after Jack tells her about the blackmail - but she is surprised by her daughter's reaction. Kim plays matchmaker for Peter and Ash, and despite the latter's reluctance they decide to go on a date - only for Suki to dampen the mood. Martin is concerned when Ruby makes it clear she wants to move on, and a talent scout appears in Walford

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Previously unseen material from the comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Former champions Irini Tzortzoglou and Jane Devonshire judge today's efforts, as five more contestants prepare signature meals and dishes involving their favourite ingredients. After one of the five cooks is eliminated from the contest, the remaining four must prepare a two-course meal to win one of the two remaining places in the quarter final

programme.title: Pretty Single
programme.desc: Beauty therapist and single mother Séan never leaves the house without make-up, so she embarks on a series of dates completely fresh-faced, hoping to find a love that is more than skin deep

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: RuPaul's search for the UK's Next Drag Race Superstar continues. This week, the queens face one of their toughest challenge yet as they are asked to write and perform their own comedy stand-up routines. Comedy legend Dawn French joins Michelle Visage and Alan Carr on the judging panel as another queen takes her leave…

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Line of Duty stars Vicky McClure and Adrian Dunbar talk about the hotly awaited sixth series of the BBC's crime drama. Star Wars' Daisy Ridley chats about starring in sci-fi adventure Chaos Walking, Breaking Bad's Bryan Cranston reflects on his role as a New Orleans judge in the TV drama Your Honor and Brit star Chiwetel Ejiofor promotes his romantic comedy heist, Locked Down. Plus, Imelda May performs Made to Love

programme.desc: Barra Best continues his journey along the Foyle river network, taking him along three counties - Donegal, Tyrone and Londonderry, to Lough Foyle. Here he joins a group practising ice water therapy on the River Roe and gets an icy surprise when his instructor decides the river is not cold enough. He also follows the journey of the salmon up Donegal's River Finn and visits the Co Tyrone town of Strabane where he meets some of those forced to flee their homes the night the River Mourne burst its banks

programme.title: Tenable

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Ross, Carmel, Samta and Aliyu, who have never met before, pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Gabby is horrified as realisation sinks in, Ellis questions Priya's motives, and Nicola resolves to take back control

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve reacts angrily when Peter lets him down. Ken offers Simon a sympathetic ear. Kirk falls victim to Sean's selling scheme

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: With the support of fiance Michael Brown, Preston mum Roisin Pelan is facing stage 3C cancer. Despite enduring challenging treatment, she has raised thousands for charities and is determined to see young daughter Ivy grow up. Alan Titchmarsh, David Domoney, Frances Tophill and Katie Rushworth proceed to turn their dangerous sloping garden in Preston, Lancashire, into a stunning music festival-inspired haven

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Peter fails to persuade Steve to summon medical help. Sean resigns from the factory. Simon warns Leanne not to cross Jacob

programme.desc: Piers chats to actor and director Rupert Everett, who reflects on the caustic comments he has made about several co-stars, including Madonna and Julia Roberts. He also talks about the promiscuous life he led in the 1970s and 1980s, the impact of the HIV/Aids crisis, and discusses his romantic relationships with women, including TV presenter Paula Yates

programme.title: Million Pound Pawn
programme.desc: The third and final episode explores the tricky and unpredictable nature of valuing assets. Fashion designer Nina takes her Russian artwork to Ray at Suttons and Robertson's in London with high hopes that the price of her paintings has gone up, but the contemporary art market is notoriously tricky, and Ray can only buy them if he is sure he can sell on and make a profit. Meanwhile, , pawnbroker Dan Hatfield takes on an unusual asset in Essex he has never dealt with before

Crime drama, starring Nicola Walker and Sanjeev Bhaskar

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport

programme.title: DNA Journey
programme.desc: Eight celebrities prepare to embark on a life changing journey to explore their family history using DNA technology and genealogy. This time, Jamie Redknapp and Freddie Flintoff travel around the country to discover each other's family histories. Proud Lancastrian Freddie is anxious that his DNA may lead him to Yorkshire, and there is a surprise in store when the pair arrive the Wakefield Mining Museum. Later, Jamie's family journey takes him to London's East End, where he uncovers a notorious mystery that has remained unsolved for generations

programme.desc: Kathy gets upset when Dr Khan asks her about her mental state. Viv and Vic fall out over Andy, Billy Hopwood returns to Emmerdale, and Chris convinces Kathy to see a counsellor

programme.desc: Nick tackles a tidy burglar who insists on clearing up the houses he robs, and investigates a suspicious church organist. Starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.desc: Jean and Sid are faced with a dilemma when they notice strange goings-on next door. Vintage comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: When Larry decides to use someone else's name instead of his own, Robin finds himself entangled in a love triangle. Comedy series, with Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Richard Abernethie is found dead shortly after disinheriting his sole heir and specifying that his legacy is to be divided equally among the remaining family members. Poirot is called in by his solicitor friend Gilbert Entwhistle, to ascertain whether the late Mr Abernethie met with foul play - and also finds time to investigate the murder of a beneficiary under the new terms of the will. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Robert Bathurst, Anna Calder-Marshall and Geraldine James

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A CND rally at the Early Warning Installation creates chaos in Aidensfield, causing Nick to fall out with Kate and an MP to be blackmailed. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Billy tells Andy he is on the run and is badly injured. Butch vows to sort Paddy out. Kathy confides to the psychologist about her feelings

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy is upset when Paddy refuses to talk to her. Andy tells Billy that he doesn't want to go to Amsterdam. Mandy confronts Butch

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The police question Les about Des's smashed window. Chris is frustrated that he can only see Sally in secret. Jack masquerades as Ken and meets one of his escort clients

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre is thrilled when Blanche and Tracy arrive for her engagement party. Sally tells Chris he's pushing their relationship too fast. Steve clears his things out of number 11

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The father of a missing schoolgirl demands action, angry that a six-month investigation has failed to uncover any leads. Morse ends up being assigned the case - and discovers some unpleasant truths about her family and teachers during the course of his inquiries. John Thaw stars as the Oxford detective, with Kevin Whately, Peter Woodthorpe, Peter McEnery, Elizabeth Hurley and Frances Tomelty

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) and four known killers are among those who meet Poirot at a bridge evening arranged by wealthy adventurer Shaitana, who sits out the first game - only to be found later stabbed through the heart. As nobody has left or entered the victim's apartment during the night, the guests combine their skills to discover which of the party is responsible. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Lesley Manville and Alex Jennings

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An aristocrat's wealthy, overbearing wife finances a North African archaeological dig to find the skull of John the Baptist. Poirot is in attendance, but when the woman is found stabbed to death, and only her husband, the exuberant Lord Boynton, appears distraught, the detective struggles to identify the killer among the wealth of suspects. Guest starring Tim Curry, Cheryl Campbell, John Hannah, Emma Cunniffe, Mark Gatiss, Angela Pleasence and Beth Goddard, with David Suchet in the title role

programme.title: Hatton Garden
programme.desc: What was supposed to be a sleepy Easter Bank holiday weekend in London's world-famous jewellery quarter is thrown into chaos, when Brian Reader leads a gang of career criminals in an attempt to carry out the daring task of breaking into the vault of the Hatton Garden Safe Deposit Company. Fact-based drama about the April 2015 heist, starring Timothy Spall, Kenneth Cranham, David Hayman and Alex Norton

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Janet comes face to face with the man who left her fighting for life just months earlier - serial killer Geoff Hastings, who has vowed to finally reveal the full extent of his crimes. Rachel and the rest of the team investigate the racially motivated murder of a taxi driver. On the personal front, Andy refuses to take no for an answer, so Janet threatens to report him to the DCI. Crime drama, starring Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones, with an appearance by Kevin Doyle (Downton Abbey's Molesley)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The investigation of a burglary at a quarry turns into something far more serious when a security guard's badly beaten body is discovered at the scene of the crime. An autistic teenager is arrested for the murder, despite Inspector Frost's misgivings - a move which triggers a chain of tragic events culminating in a tense stand-off. Crime drama, starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, Caroline Harker and Maurice Roeves

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre is thrilled when Blanche and Tracy arrive for her engagement party. Sally tells Chris he's pushing their relationship too fast. Steve clears his things out of number 11

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Billy tells Andy he is on the run and is badly injured. Butch vows to sort Paddy out. Kathy confides to the psychologist about her feelings

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy is upset when Paddy refuses to talk to her. Andy tells Billy that he doesn't want to go to Amsterdam. Mandy confronts Butch

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A CND rally at the Early Warning Installation creates chaos in Aidensfield, causing Nick to fall out with Kate and an MP to be blackmailed. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid's cousin visits the Abbott household, and the bemused family have a terrible time getting rid of him. Sid James stars

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy's mother makes plans for her daughter's future happiness - and she knows just the man to help. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) and four known killers are among those who meet Poirot at a bridge evening arranged by wealthy adventurer Shaitana, who sits out the first game - only to be found later stabbed through the heart. As nobody has left or entered the victim's apartment during the night, the guests combine their skills to discover which of the party is responsible. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Lesley Manville and Alex Jennings

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Village life is disrupted by a circus family who are accused of corrupting the vicar's daughter, wrecking the shop and breaking into the church. Meanwhile, Nick and Kate are attacked for resisting local bigots. Eleanor Bron guest stars, with Nick Berry and Niamh Cusack

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris suggests to Kathy that she comes to stay with him at Home Farm. Zak and Lisa argue about Butch. Donna pesters Andy to find out why he's been acting strangely

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Andy steals some money from the post office. Rachel cannot forgive or forget Kathy's insults. Mandy tells Paddy that Butch is a liar

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre tells Liz she gives off the wrong impression to men. Judy is touched when Rita opens a savings account for Katie. Des is furious when his house is vandalised again

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Zoe assures the Malletts she hasn't come to cause trouble. Jack is horrified when Renee tracks him down. Sally tells Rita that she's been seeing Chris

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective faces a tough time while looking into a murder at a peaceful college. After suffering the familiar pangs of unrequited love, he clashes with the unpleasant college master and then unwittingly provides his main suspects with a watertight alibi. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Robert Stephens, Amanda Burton and Philip Middlemiss

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Millionaire Gordon Cloade's wife is widowed for the second time at the tender age of 23, arousing the suspicions of his impoverished relatives. Fanning the flames of envy and hatred, psychic aunt Kathy Cloade reveals the grieving widow's first husband is in fact still alive, and a second death prompts Poirot to delve deeper into the family's dark secrets. David Suchet stars, with Jenny Agutter, Tim Piggot-Smith and Celia Imrie

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A comic convention is held in Carver Valley, but the event gets off to a sinister start when the latest creation of a mysterious writer is stolen from the organiser's house and burned. Barnaby takes up the case, which turns murderous when a former supermodel is killed during the event. The only clue is an unusual issue of the missing comics, which seems to reveal the dark secrets of six local people - and provides the police with six likely suspects. Guest starring Bill Bailey and Jemma Redgrave

programme.title: Hatton Garden
programme.desc: Returning for a second attempt at the Hatton Garden safe deposit vault, the gang members, now led by Terry Perkins, set out to finish what they started. They must obtain new equipment and get back into the building, but time is getting tight with the bank holiday weekend slipping by. Drama, starring Timothy Spall and Kenneth Cranham

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Rachel meets Sean's son, who turns out to be an eight-year-old version of his dad, and the idea of becoming a stepmum puts her further off the idea of marriage than she was before - not that she even agreed in the first place. Andy asks Janet out for a drink, but when she turns him down he accuses her of leading him on. A woman in a sexy cop's uniform turns up dead, and the DCI assumes the killer was her lover - the question is, which one of her many lovers was it? And to add to the mystery, why was her face painted green?

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack learns he is tipped for promotion with the uniform branch and even more flattered to discover he has a secret admirer, but before he has time to bask in this rare moment of glory a child murder claims his attention. Linking the disappearance of a 10-year-old boy to a mysterious hit-and-run killing, he is soon mired in a complex investigation and fuming about a bureaucratic blunder that leaves a known paedophile walking free on his patch. David Jason stars

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: When his boss goes on holiday, Sid assumes responsibility for guarding his property - but has trouble enforcing his regime. Comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Owing to a little white lie on his income tax returns, Mr Roper must quickly acquire a son. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Millionaire Gordon Cloade's wife is widowed for the second time at the tender age of 23, arousing the suspicions of his impoverished relatives. Fanning the flames of envy and hatred, psychic aunt Kathy Cloade reveals the grieving widow's first husband is in fact still alive, and a second death prompts Poirot to delve deeper into the family's dark secrets. David Suchet stars, with Jenny Agutter, Tim Piggot-Smith and Celia Imrie

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick is seconded to a Scotland Yard team who have arrived in Aidensfield to investigate the fatal shooting of former policeman Andrew Gerard, and risks his career to ensure justice is done. Meanwhile, local man Alan Maskell is duped into helping a couple of crooks respray stolen cars. Nick Berry stars

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Butch is full of remorse after attacking Marlon. Billy enlists Andy's help to steal more money. Chris is shocked to see Eric competing against him at the auction

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Lisa wants Zak to invite Marlon to Christmas dinner so they can all make a fresh start. Billy loads his shotgun ready for his Christmas Day robbery plan

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The Malletts look forward to their new life in Newcastle. Chris tells Sally he wants to move in with her. Des reluctantly thanks Les for coming to his rescue

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Natalie wants Kevin to find out about Sally and Chris. Alec asks Jack to escort for him with a new client. Maxine is stunned when Audrey tells her she intends to buy the salon

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The badly beaten body of 18-year-old clerk Sylvia Kane is found in a pub car park by her boyfriend John Sanders. Called in to investigate, Morse finds a coded letter in her bag and sets about trying to decipher it, gradually unravelling a tangled web of hidden passion and corruption at the insurance office where she worked. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Anthony Bate, Terrence Hardiman, Jill Baker, Holly Aird and Ingrid Lacey

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: In Dover, Lieutenant Colin Race is investigating the deaths of two Navy personnel when a distraught Sheila Webb runs out of 19 Wilbraham Crescent and into his arms. Inside the house, Colin finds the body of a murder victim surrounded by stopped clocks. Mystery, starring David Suchet, Anna Massey, Jaime Winstone, Lesley Sharp, Phil Daniels, Geoffrey Palmer and Tessa Peake-Jones

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: As the sleuth studies for his forthcoming sergeant's exam, he investigates a hit-and-run that claimed the life of an eminent Oxford professor. The victim had been at odds with his peers over the fate of a potentially lucrative parcel of college-owned land, and the case is complicated by the appearance of DI Thursday's old gangland enemy - whom he must face alone while Endeavour makes a trip back to Lincolnshire to visit his ailing father

programme.title: Hatton Garden
programme.desc: When the Metropolitan Police discover the burglary after the long Easter weekend, the gang members already have a two-day lead on them. They are desperate to split up the stolen goods, but more delays frustrate them. However, they soon come to realise that it is not only the police who are chasing them - Brian Reader also wants his share

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: A gangland killing takes a surprising twist when it turns out the victim has been sexually mutilated. Andy punishes Janet by keeping her out of the heart of the investigation, and when she rebels, the ensuing row becomes personal. Rachel feels ambushed by Sean, who announces his mother wants to meet her - especially as she has spent years trying not to think about her own mum. Suranne Jones, Lesley Sharp, Sean Maguire and Nicholas Gleaves star

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack returns to duty after a period of suspension, and finds himself investigating the murder of a man who had been leading a double life. In addition, he also has to deal with the baffling case of a man found dead with his right foot hacked off and replaced by a severed left foot. Drama, starring David Jason and Amanda Root

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Omnibus. Moira begs Faith, Will tells Dawn about Gabby, and Paul gets what he wants

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gary warns Zoe to stay away from them, Deirdre and Jon move into their new home, Des warns Les to stay away from Samantha, and Leanne is furious when Nick snubs her

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Des finds his car windows smashed in and believes Les did it. Fred is horrified to learn Ashley has employed Maud, and Judy asks Des and Samantha to be Katie's godparents

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris discovers that Home Farm will be sold at auction. Butch catches Paddy and Mandy together on her bed, and Kathy breaks down in front of Biff

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy gets upset when Dr Khan asks her about her mental state. Viv and Vic fall out over Andy, Billy Hopwood returns to Emmerdale, and Chris convinces Kathy to see a counsellor

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick tackles a tidy burglar who insists on clearing up the houses he robs, and investigates a suspicious church organist. Starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean and Sid are faced with a dilemma when they notice strange goings-on next door. Vintage comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: When Larry decides to use someone else's name instead of his own, Robin finds himself entangled in a love triangle. Comedy series, with Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Richard Abernethie is found dead shortly after disinheriting his sole heir and specifying that his legacy is to be divided equally among the remaining family members. Poirot is called in by his solicitor friend Gilbert Entwhistle, to ascertain whether the late Mr Abernethie met with foul play - and also finds time to investigate the murder of a beneficiary under the new terms of the will. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Robert Bathurst, Anna Calder-Marshall and Geraldine James

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A CND rally at the Early Warning Installation creates chaos in Aidensfield, causing Nick to fall out with Kate and an MP to be blackmailed. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Billy tells Andy he is on the run and is badly injured. Butch vows to sort Paddy out. Kathy confides to the psychologist about her feelings

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy is upset when Paddy refuses to talk to her. Andy tells Billy that he doesn't want to go to Amsterdam. Mandy confronts Butch

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The police question Les about Des's smashed window. Chris is frustrated that he can only see Sally in secret. Jack masquerades as Ken and meets one of his escort clients

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre is thrilled when Blanche and Tracy arrive for her engagement party. Sally tells Chris he's pushing their relationship too fast. Steve clears his things out of number 11

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The father of a missing schoolgirl demands action, angry that a six-month investigation has failed to uncover any leads. Morse ends up being assigned the case - and discovers some unpleasant truths about her family and teachers during the course of his inquiries. John Thaw stars as the Oxford detective, with Kevin Whately, Peter Woodthorpe, Peter McEnery, Elizabeth Hurley and Frances Tomelty

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) and four known killers are among those who meet Poirot at a bridge evening arranged by wealthy adventurer Shaitana, who sits out the first game - only to be found later stabbed through the heart. As nobody has left or entered the victim's apartment during the night, the guests combine their skills to discover which of the party is responsible. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Lesley Manville and Alex Jennings

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An aristocrat's wealthy, overbearing wife finances a North African archaeological dig to find the skull of John the Baptist. Poirot is in attendance, but when the woman is found stabbed to death, and only her husband, the exuberant Lord Boynton, appears distraught, the detective struggles to identify the killer among the wealth of suspects. Guest starring Tim Curry, Cheryl Campbell, John Hannah, Emma Cunniffe, Mark Gatiss, Angela Pleasence and Beth Goddard, with David Suchet in the title role

programme.title: Hatton Garden
programme.desc: What was supposed to be a sleepy Easter Bank holiday weekend in London's world-famous jewellery quarter is thrown into chaos, when Brian Reader leads a gang of career criminals in an attempt to carry out the daring task of breaking into the vault of the Hatton Garden Safe Deposit Company. Fact-based drama about the April 2015 heist, starring Timothy Spall, Kenneth Cranham, David Hayman and Alex Norton

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Janet comes face to face with the man who left her fighting for life just months earlier - serial killer Geoff Hastings, who has vowed to finally reveal the full extent of his crimes. Rachel and the rest of the team investigate the racially motivated murder of a taxi driver. On the personal front, Andy refuses to take no for an answer, so Janet threatens to report him to the DCI. Crime drama, starring Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones, with an appearance by Kevin Doyle (Downton Abbey's Molesley)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The investigation of a burglary at a quarry turns into something far more serious when a security guard's badly beaten body is discovered at the scene of the crime. An autistic teenager is arrested for the murder, despite Inspector Frost's misgivings - a move which triggers a chain of tragic events culminating in a tense stand-off. Crime drama, starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, Caroline Harker and Maurice Roeves

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: Harry has to protect a Russian writer who has recently been smuggled into Britain

programme.title: The Casebook of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A governess is accused of shooting dead her millionaire employer's wife on a remote bridge - but Holmes' deductive powers suggest an alternative explanation to the mystery. Period detective drama, guest starring Daniel Massey, with Jeremy Brett and Edward Hardwicke

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar is asked to take a diamond to Amsterdam for recutting - but crooks are determined to get their hands on it by any means necessary. Classic adventure, guest starring TP McKenna and Terence Rigby

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A bad dream has Regan's girlfriend fearing for his safety, and her visions ring true when he gets involved in a case involving a stolen lorryload of cigars. The plot thickens when illicit cocaine is retrieved and arms stolen from the US are discovered to be on their way to a military faction in Ireland. John Thaw and Dennis Waterman star

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Tara King finds herself the subject of an all too welcoming holiday resort with a 'no check-out' policy for some of their guests in this comedic parody of Patrick McGoohan's iconic series The Prisoner. Linda Thorson stars

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Australian private investigator Bill McCabe approaches Arthur and tells him he is the possible heir of Joshua Daley, who died intestate in Woolomoroo. Believing kinship has been proved, the dodgy dealer prepares to fly Down Under to claim what appears to be rightfully his. George Cole stars

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: A look behind the scenes at the Bombay Sapphire distillery to discover how exotic botanicals from around the world are used to flavour this classic British gin

programme.title: The Casebook of Sherlock Holmes

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A business tycoon is blackmailed for his part in a massacre while serving in the Army 25 years earlier. However, his problems are compounded after he seeks help from his former commanding officer. Colin Jeavons guest stars, with John Thaw

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed and Tara follow a chain of couriers passing a sophisticated briefcase thought to be carrying a large payoff for an unidentified traitor within the government. Penelope Keith guest stars

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur pawns his wife's jewellery to solve a temporary cash-flow problem _ but soon finds himself in deeper trouble than ever. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole as shady London entrepreneur Arthur Daley

programme.desc: How the Burton's biscuit mega-factory puts the jam into Jammie Dodgers, and the former furniture designer who is hand crafting bespoke surfboards on the Cornish coast

programme.title: Cycling: Paris-Nice Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage four, featuring a hilly 188km course from Chalon-sur-Saone to Chiroubles that saw the peloton climb Mont Brouilly twice. Gary Imlach presents, with commentary by Ned Boulting and David Millar, and reports from Daniel Friebe

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade travels to the far north of Australia to the area where a light aircraft carrying a group of aboriginal elders was forced to ditch in the sea. Six men found themselves in open water, and then disappeared - almost without trace. Only a single severed leg was ever recovered, but what is in these waters that can take out half a dozen capable men leaving barely a clue?

programme.title: Giant Lobster Hunters
programme.desc: The skippers reveal their secrets as they take viewers inside their world with engine fails, new technology, expensive machinery and epic sea disasters

programme.title: Space Cowboys (2000)
programme.desc: A former Air Force test pilot, who was sidelined from space exploration because of his difficult character, is recruited 40 years later to help America's post-Cold War Russian allies. Teaming up with three fellow veteran airmen, he uses his expertise in navigation to stop a malfunctioning Soviet satellite from crashing to Earth. Adventure, directed by and starring Clint Eastwood. With Tommy Lee Jones, James Garner, Donald Sutherland and James Cromwell

Six men found themselves in open water, and then disappeared - almost without trace. Only a single severed leg was ever recovered, but what is in these waters that can take out half a dozen capable men leaving barely a clue?

programme.title: Space Cowboys (2000)
programme.desc: A former Air Force test pilot, who was sidelined from space exploration because of his difficult character, is recruited 40 years later to help America's post-Cold War Russian allies. Teaming up with three fellow veteran airmen, he uses his expertise in navigation to stop a malfunctioning Soviet satellite from crashing to Earth. Adventure, directed by and starring Clint Eastwood. With Tommy Lee Jones, James Garner, Donald Sutherland and James Cromwell

programme.title: Dispatches
programme.desc: Environmental journalist Lucy Siegle investigates the rise of waste incineration in the UK, examining how millions of tonnes of waste left out for recycling ends up being burned. The rise in carbon emissions from incineration is put under the spotlight, with exclusive research revealing that it is on course to become the UK's dirtiest form of energy production. The programme also looks at how councils are locked into expensive contracts with incinerator operators forcing them to burn waste for decades to come

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The documentary follows the work of the stroke team at south London's King's College Hospital. programme.title: Our Baby: A Modern Miracle
programme.desc: Documentary following transgender couple Hannah and Jake Graf as they start a family. Filmed over the course of a year, the programme follows Hannah and Jake from their first meeting with a surrogate through to their baby's birth in the midst of lockdown. The film also explores their experiences of transitioning and the prejudice they've faced, as they strive to realise their dream of becoming parents

programme.title: The Undateables
programme.desc: Emily, who has a learning disability, is determined to find her first proper boyfriend, but she has to overcome her nerves and her tendency to talk too much. Meanwhile, Poetry lover Michael, who has Asperger's, has been single for 14 years and hopes he can one day find love, and dance teacher Tobi is fearless on the dance floor, but is something of a novice when it comes to romance

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to Charlie's Italian bistro in La Verne, southern California, where the owner's lack of business nous is putting her relatives' homes at risk, after her mother and sister put them up as collateral. To make matters worse, the restaurant's food is less than appetising and its long-serving chef seems to be incompetent

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Colin and Mandy kick proceedings off in Bristol with a colourful and spicy assortment of courses, while their guests are treated to a night of conversation about romantic meetings, outrageous shirts and naked gardening. Next, PJ and Owen defy expectations and create a fancy menu, but their purple potato mash is more like play-doh, while Geoff and Gemma round things off with a hearty and rich selection of courses. Who will secure the £1000 prize?

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick treats a Persian cat that has been hit by a car, requiring treatments involving a skin graft and putting its crushed bones back together. Meanwhile, a rescue dog has a cancerous tumour in his jaw removed, and a young staffie-cross needs treatment for a developmental elbow disease

programme.title: Fargo
programme.desc: Nicki is arrested in connection with Ray's death and is questioned by Chief Dammik, who suspects that she killed him in retaliation for abuse, while Gloria asks Winnie to tell Emmit about his brother and gauge his reaction. Black comedy drama, starring Ewan McGregor and Carrie Coon

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: The host visits Ben at his ground floor apartment in St Leonards-on-Sea, East Sussex. George's challenge is to turn a grand drawing room with sea views into a cool, elegant kitchen-diner and create rich, opulent schemes for a bedroom and study. In Manchester, he tackles a 1930s semi-detached where the compact dimensions are proving a headache. With only a tiny budget to work with, can he make a cramped galley kitchen both functional and beautiful?

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: The charity version of the popular baking show returns, and with Noel Fielding on paternity leave, Matt Lucas is presenting on his own. In previous editions, comedian Tom Allen has stepped in to co-present - but today he is showing off his baking skills as one of the contestants, alongside Star Wars actress Daisy Ridley, comedian Rob Beckett and singer Alexandra Burke. To impress Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith, the celebrities serve up millionaire shortbread, a fruity tart and their pet hates in cake form in the showstopper

programme.title: The Celebrity Circle for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: A group of famous faces move into the apartment block where they communicate only by social media. The name of the game is to become the most popular player, and while most of them know who they are chatting to, a few are hiding behind fake profiles. The game begins with Denise van Outen, Duncan James, Lady Leshurr, Saffron Barker, Baga Chipz, Kaye Adams and Nadia Sawalha, Rickie Haywood-Williams and Melvin Odoom, and Sam Thompson and Pete Wicks. They give their first impressions, and presenter Emma Willis delivers big news to the players

programme.title: The Sex Clinic
programme.desc: Student Taps comes to the clinic as he has never been able to ejaculate during sex, and he's hoping that the resident therapist will help him overcome his problem. Chloe from Cardiff wants advice on 'pegging', and personal trainer Tracy is concerned that she might be addicted to sex. Finally, barman Tom needs an STI test and Jordan is worried about a lump on his bum

programme.title: Let's Talk About Sex
programme.desc: The first of three programmes drawing on contemporary and archive footage to explore the nation's changing attitude to sex and sex education through the ages. It features parents and their children - including celebrities with their offspring - as they watch, review and react to outrageous, astounding and unintentionally hilarious sex education videos covering everything from puberty to pregnancy. The first episode focuses on how sex education handles basic biology, from managing menstruation to the rules of reproduction and the challenges of masturbation. Contributors include Danny Dyer, Miriam Margolyes, Alastair Campbell, London Hughes and Ulrika Jonsson

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Baltimore, Maryland, where Denise Whiting has alienated customers by trademarking the word 'hon', a term of endearment used by the local community. When she opened Cafe Hon in 1992 it was a roaring success, but as a result of Denise's unwavering business ambition, people started to boycott the restaurant, leaving her frustrated by falling sales

programme.title: The Great Pottery Throw Down

In Larne in Northern Ireland, Catherine and Nigel Finch have hit a spot of turbulence due to their dated, 1970s-style home. Nigel enjoys the rural location's incredible views, but Catherine has never loved the place and misses having neighbours, so Phil searches for a home that pleases them both

The film also discovers how the country dealt with the aftermath of these natural disasters, as people struggled to rebuild their lives and communities

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Forty-three-year-old Mohammad, who has motor neurone disease, is rushed to St George's with severe sepsis and doctors work to get the infection under control, while David has fallen 20 feet from a billboard and the trauma team is concerned that the 47-year-old has suffered life-changing injuries. Retired journalist Tom is in the urgent care centre after knocking his arm and his wife Eileen reveals how their plans have been turned upside down due to an unexpected bombshell six weeks previously

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: Cornish adaptive surfer Pegleg plans to extend his VW campervan by creating a new cabin from scratch, while Martin and Carol-Anne want to build a glamping pod in their Pembrokeshire garden, using a dome that originally housed an electricity generator at the London Olympics. programme.title: Dispatches
programme.desc: Environmental journalist Lucy Siegle investigates the rise of waste incineration in the UK, examining how millions of tonnes of waste left out for recycling ends up being burned. The rise in carbon emissions from incineration is put under the spotlight, with exclusive research revealing that it is on course to become the UK's dirtiest form of energy production. The programme also looks at how councils are locked into expensive contracts with incinerator operators forcing them to burn waste for decades to come

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Dipi is forced to face the consequences of her actions. Hendrix, unable to take a hint, arranges another horribly misguided romantic gesture towards Harlow

programme.title: The World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: The stories of six epic train journeys, seen through the characters who travel, work and live near them. The first programme rides through Canada's iconic Rocky Mountaineer train in a jaw-dropping loop from Vancouver to the heart of the Rockies, through some of North America's most staggering natural beauty, and on to Banff in Alberta. The journey also ventures through the spiral tunnels and shows how to track bears with the National Park rangers. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Documentary featuring exclusive access to Nottinghamshire's traffic units. The firearms team mobilises to tackle a suspect armed with a pistol, and hours into a nerve-shredding siege, the man emerges with a handgun and fires. Elsewhere, interceptors Rob and Dan join in the pursuit of a runaway hatchback, while the members of the knife crime team track down a suspect on a bike trying to evade capture in a park

programme.title: The Killing of James Bulger
programme.desc: On 12th February 1993, a CCTV camera captured footage of two young boys leading a two-year-old child away from the Strand shopping centre in Liverpool. This two-part documentary tells the definitive story of the case, examining in forensic detail what happened on that tragic day, how the young killers were caught and the devastating legacy of the murder on James's family and the community. With access to his mother Denise Fergus, his brothers speaking exclusively for the first time, along with the police and others closely involved in the case

programme.title: Medical Mysteries
programme.desc: Documentary featuring more patients in search of answers for their mysterious conditions. Alicia was in her first term at university when she started to suffer uncontrollable tremors, making it impossible for her to walk, talk and write. Nineteen-year-old Elizabeth's body changed dramatically within 18 months as her weight almost doubled and she developed facial hair and a hump on her back, while air stewardess Dee had to leave her job of 25 years when she developed an unexplained sickness

programme.title: Dale Cregan: Hunting a Cop Killer
programme.desc: Documentary looking back at the hunt for the serial killer Dale Cregan in Manchester in 2012, who killed four people, two of which were female police officers. After murdering two men linked to a rival family in quick succession, Cregan lured two innocent female officers to his house in Hattersley, Tameside, where he shot them multiple times before fleeing from the scene and eventually handing himself in to the police

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Nick Wallis examines how CCTV means wrongdoers can no longer hide in a crowd, looking at its use in convicting people involved in the August 2011 riots across England

programme.title: BSL Zone

programme.title: 12 Angry Men (1957)
programme.desc: Twelve jurors convene to decide the verdict in a murder trial. While 11 of the group are thoroughly convinced the teenage defendant is guilty, one lone voice of reason refuses to cave in to pressure and passionately argues the case for his innocence. Sidney Lumet's legal drama, starring Henry Fonda, Lee J Cobb, Ed Begley, Martin Balsam and Jack Klugman

programme.title: 3:10 to Yuma (1957)
programme.desc: A farmer in need of money agrees to guard a notorious outlaw until the train arrives to transport him to jail. Meanwhile, the pair are holed up in a hotel, where his prisoner plays mind games with him - and the villain's cohorts are waiting outside ready to engineer an escape. Western adapted from a novel by Elmore Leonard, with Van Heflin, Glenn Ford, Felicia Farr and Leora Dana

programme.title: The Stranger (1946)
programme.desc: A war crimes investigator is on the trail of an infamous Nazi. Drama, directed by and starring Orson Welles, with Loretta Young

programme.title: The Riddle of the Sands (1979)
programme.desc: An English yachtsman sailing round the coast of Germany in 1901 encounters a naval officer who has betrayed his country. Enlisting the aid of a friend, he discovers a plan by Kaiser Wilhelm to invade Britain - which they are the only ones in a position to stop. Spy thriller, starring Simon MacCorkindale, Michael York and Jenny Agutter

programme.title: Belle (2013)
programme.desc: The illegitimate mixed-race daughter of a Royal Navy admiral is raised in the household of her great uncle, but is never entirely accepted in Georgian British society. She falls in love with an idealistic lawyer, and together they campaign against the slave trade. Fact-based period drama, starring Gugu Mbatha-Raw, Tom Wilkinson and Emily Watson

programme.title: Hidden Figures (2016)
programme.desc: As the space race reaches fever pitch in the early 1960s, a trio of female African-American mathematicians attempt to overcome prejudice and find work with NASA. Their remarkable skills prove vital in getting an astronaut into orbit and bringing him safely back to Earth, but to realise their potential they must overcome deep-seated prejudice in the space programme and society as a whole. Fact-based drama, starring Taraji P Henson, Octavia Spencer, Janelle Monae and Kevin Costner

programme.title: The Dressmaker (2015)
programme.desc: In 1950s Australia, a fashion designer returns to her remote home town after forging a successful career in Paris. She reconciles with her mother, falls in love and transforms the lives of the local women - getting even with her enemies in the process. Drama based on the novel by Rosalie Ham, starring Kate Winslet, Judy Davis, Hugo Weaving and Liam Hemsworth

programme.title: Skate Kitchen (2018)
programme.desc: Teenage skateboarder Camille's life as a lonely suburban teenager changes dramatically when she befriends a bunch of other skateboarding girls in New York City. As she journeys deeper into the city's subculture, she begins to understand the true meaning of friendship. Drama, starring Rachelle Vinberg, Dede Lovelace and Nina Moran

programme.title: Legend of the Lost (1957)
programme.desc: A grizzled adventurer living in Timbuktu is hired to escort a mysterious explorer across the Sahara desert in search of a fabled lost city. A local woman joins the men on their quest, but when the trio reach their destination the explorer reveals his plan to betray his companions and leave them high and dry. Adventure, starring John Wayne, Sophia Loren and Rossano Brazzi

programme.title: A Day to Remember (1953)
programme.desc: The members of the darts team from the Hand & Flower pub in London head off on a day trip to Boulogne. Among the group is Jim, who is worried that his girlfriend plans to leave him, Shorty, a loner who hopes to join the Foreign Legion, the woman-chasing Charley, and Stan, an entrepreneur who wants to smuggle black-market watches back to the UK. Comedy, starring Stanley Holloway, Donald Sinden and Joan Rice

programme.title: Samson and Delilah (1949)
programme.desc: Hebrew warrior Samson becomes a wanted man after crossing paths with Philistine troops, but he constantly thwarts their efforts to capture him using to his incredible strength. Determined to bring him down, the Saran of Gaza makes use of his beguiling sister Delilah, who seduces Samson and manages to rob him of the source of his power. Cecil B DeMille's Oscar-winning adaptation of the biblical tale, starring Victor Mature, Hedy Lamarr, George Sanders, Angela Lansbury and Henry Wilcox

programme.title: Tomahawk (1951)
programme.desc: Frontier scout Jim Bridger doesn't approve when a gold rush leads the US army to build a fort on land occupied by the Sioux. The situation goes from bad to worse when a sadistic officer launches an unprovoked attack, prompting a vengeful tribe to go on the offensive and leaving Bridger the only one who can save the soldiers at the fort from certain death. Western, starring Van Heflin, Yvonne de Carlo and Alex Nicol, and featuring an early appearance by Rock Hudson

programme.title: Legend (1985)
programme.desc: A peasant boy, who has a special bond with animals and fairies, shows his sweetheart the last surviving unicorns. Unfortunately, a monstrous demon and his goblin henchmen despise the mythical creatures and trick the girl into harming one of them, forcing Jack to act quickly to save her immortal soul. Ridley Scott's fantasy adventure, starring Tom Cruise, Mia Sara, Tim Curry and David Bennent

programme.title: Dawn of the Planet of the Apes (2014)
programme.desc: A colony of super-intelligent apes thrives following a global virus outbreak that wipes out much of the human race. A survivor tries to make peace with the leader of the apes, but their efforts to reach an understanding are threatened by warmongers on both sides. Sci-fi thriller sequel, starring Andy Serkis, Jason Clarke and Gary Oldman

programme.title: Stratton (2017)
programme.desc: A British Special Boat Service commando tracks down an international terrorist cell. Thriller, starring Dominic Cooper, Austin Stowell and Gemma Chan

programme.title: Neds (2010)
programme.desc: A teenager in 1970s Glasgow is eager to get a good education, but finds his older brother's reputation as a brutal thug an obstacle, as everyone expects him to turn out the same way. He ultimately conforms to their expectations by falling in with a violent street gang. Peter Mullan's drama, starring Conor McCarron, Gregg Forrest and Richard Mack

programme.title: Pony Express (1953)
programme.desc: Buffalo Bill Cody and Wild Bill Hickok work on establishing an east-west route for the pioneering mail service that will allow messages to be sent from Missouri to California in 10 days. However, they have to contend with saboteurs planning to prevent California becoming part of the Union. Western, starring Charlton Heston, Forrest Tucker, Rhonda Fleming and Jan Sterling

programme.title: The Long Memory (1952)
programme.desc: A man is released from prison after serving a 12-year sentence for a crime he did not commit, and immediately sets about tracking down three witnesses who lied in court, bringing about his conviction. However, while seeking revenge, he discovers the case is more complicated than expected. Thriller, starring John Mills, Elizabeth Sellars, John McCallum and Geoffrey Keen

programme.title: Detective Story (1951)
programme.desc: A moralistic New York cop is determined to ensure the conviction of an illegal abortionist implicated in the deaths of several women. However, his obsession leads him to lose control, and he is disturbed to discover his own wife was once one of the doctor's patients - a secret she had kept from him. Police drama, starring Kirk Douglas, Eleanor Parker and William Bendix

programme.title: Apache Uprising (1965)
programme.desc: Western, starring Rory Calhoun as a tough-talking cowboy with a heart of gold who bravely defends fellow stagecoach passengers from a potentially lethal Indian attack. Also starring Corinne Calvet, Lon Chaney Jnr and John Russell

programme.title: Home Alone (1990)
programme.desc: A boy has to fend for himself when he is accidentally left behind after his family jets off to Paris for Christmas. The youngster initially enjoys his freedom, but is soon forced to resort to ingenious booby traps to defend the family home from two dim-witted burglars. Comedy, starring Macaulay Culkin, Joe Pesci, Daniel Stern, John Heard, Catherine O'Hara and John Candy

programme.title: Taken (2008)
programme.desc: An American teenager travelling through Europe is abducted by a gang of sex traffickers. However, what the kidnappers do not realise is that the girl's father is a former CIA agent, who employs all his old skills as he searches for the criminals and tries to rescue his daughter. Thriller, starring Liam Neeson, Famke Janssen and Maggie Grace

programme.title: Cowboys & Aliens (2011)
programme.desc: A Wild-West town dominated by a cattle baron comes under attack by invaders from another world. It falls to a mysterious stranger with no memory to unite the townsfolk, a gang of outlaws and an Apache tribe, in a bid to fight back against the aliens, and learn the truth about his own past. Sci-fi Western, with Daniel Craig, Harrison Ford, Olivia Wilde and Sam Rockwell

programme.title: Eden (2014)
programme.desc: A literature student abandons his thesis as his DJing career takes off, achieving success in US clubs, but his spending and cocaine habit start catching up with him. Drama, starring Felix de Givry and Pauline Etienne. In French and English

programme.desc: Leonard is depressed that everyone seems to be paired off except him, and resorts to desperate measures to get a girlfriend. Meanwhile, Sheldon looks for a way out of his relationship with Amy, who wants him to meet her mother. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki and Jim Parsons

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Fresh from the success of hosting a three-day wedding at the chateau, Dick and Angel make business plans for the grounds, intending to charge people to fish in the moat and forage in the woods. Angel starts work on a second guest suite, but also wants to install an elevator, which pushes Dick's engineering skills to the limit

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at the Holland Hall Hotel in Upholland, Lancashire, where two of the guests are less than impressed when the breakfast table is laid with steak knives

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien and Henry Cole visit Witney in Oxfordshire, where Henry comes across a tractor that has not run for quite a few years - but poses no problem for restorer Alan

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Visayan warty pig Wendy is fed up with the attentions of male pig Jake and decides to escape. Meanwhile, young giraffe Narus has been battling with other young males in the herd, so the keepers think that it's time to move him to a new zoo. Which is easier said than done

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge celebrates his birthday with a hot-air balloon ride over the chateau at sunrise, before setting to work in the kitchen making his own jam and alcohol from the vast quantities of fresh fruit waiting to be harvested in the garden. Angel Adoree starts work on a new project of her own - building a geodesic dome on top of a floating platform

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows former blacksmith Alan Dawson, who has devised a way of building that should allow him to complete his home in just seven days. The method resembles that of a giant toy construction, with wiring, lighting, plumbing and carpets built into sections that are then taken to the site by lorry - but precision is critical if the house is to fit together

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows an ambitious build in Cornwall, as Leigh and Richard transform a derelict 17th century flour mill into a contemporary, three-bedroom home. They employ traditional craftsmen to start work on restoring the exterior, but it's not long before the true scale of the damage to the structure is revealed, and as the costs rise, they are forced to take more of the work themselves

programme.title: Britain's Most Expensive Houses
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes with UK Sotheby's International Realty, with the coronavirus pandemic having seen a surge of super-rich buyers leaving towns and cities. The brokers set out to sell a property in Highgate, north London, that is on the market for £40 million, as well the home of former Liverpool FC CEO Peter Moore on the Wirral

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Retired builder Roger has fallen 13ft from his daughter's roof and the trauma team is concerned he may have badly injured his head, back and neck. Folk musician Tad, 29, has come to A&E after developing a dangerously high heart rate, and doctors must use an electric shock to try to get it back to normal. Meanwhile, 69-year-old Richard is brought in after being found unwell at home by his son, and there are fears he has developed a serious infection

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
