programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman shows Sri Lankan couple Kulan and Kamala five properties for their budget of £200,000 in Portugal's Algarve region. However, it remains to be seen which property has the most 'wow' factor to become their dream holiday home

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien arrive in York, where Henry struggles to squeeze into some motorcycle leathers and Simon gets into deep water when he tries to restore an aluminium boat

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit a stately home in Somerset, where Simon is convinced he has uncovered a genuine Tudor relic

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge celebrates his birthday with a hot-air balloon ride over the chateau at sunrise, before setting to work in the kitchen making his own jam and alcohol from the vast quantities of fresh fruit waiting to be harvested in the garden. Angel Adoree starts work on a new project of her own - building a geodesic dome on top of a floating platform

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton heads to rural Buckinghamshire to help Kat Dancer with her contemporary farmhouse. Kat has always wanted to build on the farm where she grew up and Charlie has bright ideas to make the most of the idyllic plot. However, when Kat is left to manage the project alone, the whole construction hangs in the balance

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition starts in Sancton, East Yorkshire and The North Star Club, owned by Carolyn Van Outersterp, who is joined in the competition by her daughter Galatea, where they hope their peaceful woodland cabins win over their guests. On arrival, guests Luke and John are critical of the cleanliness, and the pricing of the rooms raise eyebrows. At dinner, the reveal of a self-catering breakfast ruffles some feathers, but it's the lack of wi-fi that divides the guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Friends B&B in Blackpool, Lancashire run by Luke and John Conway, where a lack of attention to detail in the rooms leaves one critic unimpressed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at The Lincoln Holiday Retreat, where guests Luke and John's great first impressions are muddied due to cleanliness concerns

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to The Chapel in Harrogate, North Yorkshire, whose owners Mark Hinchliffe and Heidi Donohoe, pride themselves on offering something a little different in their converted chapel. On arrival, the guests are wowed by the interior and artwork on show. However, Luke and John discover some grime underneath the glamour and Debbie is concerned about her traditional Chinese bed. At dinner, all the guests congratulate Mark and Heidi on their pretty property, but things soon turn ugly when talk turns to the looming payment day as it becomes clear that the B&B'ers have grievances to air

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day of the competition sees the B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. The North Star Club is first and owner Carolyn Van Outersterp has some cleanliness issues she wants to brush up on after receiving a score of one earlier in the week

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole makes a table out of a part from a jet in Tiverton, and Simon O'Brien finds a pair of exhaust pipes which he thinks could become a trendy pair of lamps

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Elderly cheetah Matrah is ostracised by his brothers Burba and Singha when he starts to show signs of a leg injury. Cameras also follow Chester Zoo's Sumatran orangutans, including adolescent female Tuti and four-month-old Kesuma. And there's also a look at Asian pond turtle Shrek, whose love life proved fatal for a previous mate

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: As Dick and Angel prepare for winter, they attend a food festival in Rouen, where they hope to find inspiration over what to do with their fresh fruit and vegetables. Angel adds the finishing touches to the floating geodesic dome in time for a family sleepover, while Dick gets to work fixing the crumbling stucco

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of Robert and Milla Gaukroger, as they try to add three extensions to a 1980s property overlooking Windermere in the Lake District. Robert has designed his own heating system to achieve maximum sustainability, but the project begins with only £100,000 of the £400,000 budget in place, and he and his family have to move into the existing building while work goes on around them

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: With no guests to entertain at the chateau this year, the overhaul of the orangery continues. All the family come together to build an open kitchen, create a pop-up restaurant and cook a menu of memories featuring dishes from Dick and Angel's years together

programme.title: Million Pound Mega Yachts
programme.desc: Documentary about the Monaco Yacht Show, where over 100 of the largest yachts on the water are up for sale, with billionaire buyers competing to own the most extravagant vessels. The programme follows those who make, buy and work on these craft, including a Soho businessman planning to sail around the world, a broker with 25 years experience looking to sell a £130 million yacht, and a billionaire installing a crystal chandelier in his floating palace

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Documentary following life inside the A&E department of St George's in south-west London. Carole, aged 45, is brought in after collapsing at home with chest pains. Tests reveal she has suffered a heart attack, so she undergoes an emergency procedure to unblock an artery. Philip, a 53-year-old psychiatrist, is rushed in following a seizure. Doctors suspect he might have suffered a stroke, but when it is revealed he was diagnosed with lung cancer several months earlier, registrar Chris believes it could be related. Finally, doctors treat 25-year-old Lucy, who is suffering severe headaches brought on by the build-up of excess spinal fluid in her head

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: With no guests to entertain at the chateau this year, the overhaul of the orangery continues. All the family come together to build an open kitchen, create a pop-up restaurant and cook a menu of memories featuring dishes from Dick and Angel's years together

programme.title: Million Pound Mega Yachts

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt investigates concerns about levels of naturally occurring arsenic in baby rice products, Jimmy Doherty examines Marks & Spencer's claim that they can trace all their beefburgers back to the cow, and Kate Quilton find out whether a parasite could be hiding in sushi. Programme exploring the secrets behind mass-produced food, meeting technicians, scientists, factory owners, growers and producers to find out exactly what goes into the groceries that end up on the supermarket shelves

Scarlette Douglas searches far and wide to find them the perfect property for their £55,000 budget. But will it be a modern apartment near the sea or a traditional cave house in the countryside that wins their hearts?

To keep costs down, Daren and Adi enlist the help of unskilled volunteers to work on the house, in exchange for teaching them the construction techniques

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The three motoring enthusiasts head to Spain on a mission to help its struggling economy - but the convertible supercars they take are very expensive and anything but discreet. With Jeremy Clarkson in a McLaren MP4-12C, Richard Hammond driving a Ferrari 458 and James May behind the wheel of an Audi R8 V10, the trio encounter mountain passes, empty towns and an abandoned airport before creating their own street circuit in a deserted Madrid development. Plus, Sherlock star Benedict Cumberbatch sets a lap time in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May embark on a project to build a car that can turn into a hovercraft and bring relief to flooded areas. Their efforts do not start smoothly, but it is not long before the trio are demonstrating the brilliance of their creation with an eventful journey down the River Avon. Plus, Jeremy grabs the keys to two variants of the Mercedes-Benz SLS AMG supercar - the V8-engined Black Series with a roaring 622bhp, and the 740bhp Electric Drive, which is powered by four electric motors. X-Men and Les Miserables star Hugh Jackman is the latest celebrity to take the new Reasonably Priced Car around the test track

programme.title: Rick Stein's Spain
programme.desc: The chef heads for the region of Extremadura, famous for paprika and Iberico ham. He then concludes his exploration of the country by driving south in his camper van to the city of Seville - the capital of tapas and flamenco

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and James May take a look at increasingly popular tall hatchbacks known as crossovers. Having concluded their primary appeal is to caravanners, the duo spend a couple of days in models by Mazda and Volkswagen and try to get a feel for what life is like for the people they believe would buy one. Meanwhile, Richard Hammond drives a pair of exciting Lamborghinis in Italy - the ferocious Aventador Roadster and the limited-edition Sesto Elemento, which carries an eye-watering price tag of around £2million. Two cars are also put through their paces on the test track, with James in a modern re-creation of the classic Porsche 911 and Aerosmith frontman Steven Tyler in the rather less exotic Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson drives the Jaguar F-Type sports car on some of Britain's finest roads, while Richard Hammond jumps inside the Range Rover Sport and gives it a thorough workout off-road, on a track and in what he believes is its most natural habitat - the lanes of Cheshire. James May goes for a countryside adventure in the New Routemaster London bus brought into use in 2013, which is propelled by a turbodiesel generator and a powerful electric motor. Inspired by the machines they have tested, the trio then meet to celebrate Britain's motor industry with a grand finale in front of Buckingham Palace. Plus, former Formula One driver Mark Webber takes to the track for a lap in the Reasonably Priced Car

The aim of the game is to hoodwink opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies - like whether Louie makes himself cry before every performance and if David actually is seeing a psychiatrist to cure a strange addiction

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy actress Katherine Parkinson, dance guru Louie Spence, comedian David O'Doherty and breakfast news presenter Bill Turnbull join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon. The aim of the game is to hoodwink opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies - like whether Louie makes himself cry before every performance and if David actually is seeing a psychiatrist to cure a strange addiction

programme.title: American Pickers

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and James May take a look at increasingly popular tall hatchbacks known as crossovers. Having concluded their primary appeal is to caravanners, the duo spend a couple of days in models by Mazda and Volkswagen and try to get a feel for what life is like for the people they believe would buy one. Meanwhile, Richard Hammond drives a pair of exciting Lamborghinis in Italy - the ferocious Aventador Roadster and the limited-edition Sesto Elemento, which carries an eye-watering price tag of around £2million. Two cars are also put through their paces on the test track, with James in a modern re-creation of the classic Porsche 911 and Aerosmith frontman Steven Tyler in the rather less exotic Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson drives the Jaguar F-Type sports car on some of Britain's finest roads, while Richard Hammond jumps inside the Range Rover Sport and gives it a thorough workout off-road, on a track and in what he believes is its most natural habitat - the lanes of Cheshire. James May goes for a countryside adventure in the New Routemaster London bus brought into use in 2013, which is propelled by a turbodiesel generator and a powerful electric motor. Inspired by the machines they have tested, the trio then meet to celebrate Britain's motor industry with a grand finale in front of Buckingham Palace. Plus, former Formula One driver Mark Webber takes to the track for a lap in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Rick Stein's India
programme.desc: The chef begins an exploration of the country's food in the states of West Bengal and Tamil Nadu, where the influence of the British Empire on eating habits can still be observed. Rick enjoys the prominence of fish and seafood in the cuisine found in the cities of Kolkata and Chennai, cooking dishes including prawns in coconut milk and his mum's British Raj curry

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May are in Russia as the motoring magazine returns, taking part in a race across the urban landscape of St Petersburg. James defends the honour of the car in a very small Renault, Richard tries to prove that pedalling is faster by riding a bicycle, while Jeremy attempts to demonstrate that water is the quickest way across town by piloting a hovercraft. To make matters more complicated, all three presenters are racing not only one another but also the Stig, who is making his way toward the finish line by public transport. Plus, the new Lamborghini Huracan is put through its paces and singer-songwriter Ed Sheeran takes to the test track in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The presenters embark on a road trip across Australia's Northern Territory in fast, luxurious GT cars, with Jeremy Clarkson choosing a BMW M6 Gran Coupe, Richard Hammond picking a Bentley Continental GT V8 and James May opting for a Nissan GT-R. The trio set out on an adventure that takes in crocodile-infested rivers, a race around a vast opencast mine and an encounter with one of the world's longest lorries, concluding with an unusual challenge involving a farm, their cars and 4,000 cows. Plus, 24 star Kiefer Sutherland takes to the track in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: The competitors are lured to a wasteland for a challenge involving eggs and medical equipment. David Baddiel spray-paints his face and Jo Brand reveals her psychic superpowers. Presented by Greg Davies, with help from co-host Alex Horne

programme.title: Unearthing WWII
programme.desc: Military historian David O'Keefe and filmmaker Wayne Abbott explore the site of Operation Market Garden

programme.title: Unearthing WWII
programme.desc: Relics of the Rhineland Campaign of March 1945, which was the first Allied push into Nazi Germany

programme.title: Royal Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: The mysterious death of the Duke of Kent in a 1942 plane crash, which has led to accusations of the Royal family erasing him from history

programme.title: Underground Worlds
programme.desc: Incredible feats of human endeavour, revealing what people have built, where they've built it, how and why. The first edition explores deep inside the rock on Gibraltar where there's over 55km complex of tunnels, under London, where 21st-century engineering innovation is paving the way for the ultimate super sewer and continues the legacy of Victorian pioneers and reveals the extraordinary subterranean space of one of Europe's largest and most breath-taking natural cave systems - The Postojna Cave in Slovenia

programme.title: Underground Worlds
programme.desc: A look at Sweden's passion for subterranean spaces and how this nation has taken extraordinary precautions to protect itself - by excavating an astounding 65,000 nuclear bunkers. In south-west England, deep in the Mendip Hills of Somerset, lies one of the most spectacular landscapes in the UK, but beneath this breath-taking landscape sits secret caverns and magical rock formations carved out by nature. Plus, how in Romania, nside a 1.2km-thick layer of salt, sits a world underground first

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On the next leg of his journey, Michael Portillo heads along the Essex bank of the Thames before crossing the river into Kent. He begins in Barkingside, where Victorian philanthropist Thomas Barnardo made it his mission to transform the lives of destitute children. The former politician then tries his hand at loading a container onto a pocket wagon at Tilbury docks, and ends his trip in Rochester, where he encounters a host of familiar characters and explores a town that was an inspiration for many of Charles Dickens' works

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo starts the next leg of his journey to Chichester in Faversham, Kent, where he visits Shepherd Neame, which is one of the oldest breweries in Britain. He then heads to Dover to explore a sunken fortress known as the Western Heights, before crossing into Surrey where he blow-dries a hen in Dorking

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team head to the Royal Hall in Harrogate, with Alastair Dickenson giving his highest valuation in 20 years on the show. Plus, the emotional story of a sapphire ring left to a young man by a recently departed friend, and the family history behind Betty's Tea Rooms

programme.title: Impossible Railways
programme.desc: How some of our greatest railways have challenged engineering's finest minds in their efforts to conquer the planet's waterways, including a tunnel beneath the English Channel.

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: Documentary following the exploits of a family-run crew of heavy-haulage specialists as they transport the heaviest, longest and most precious locomotives around the world. Terry and Jason have been called in to move legendary steam locomotive Britannia. The 68-year-old vehicle has undergone a major restoration in Crewe and is scheduled to start running at the Severn Valley Railway

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A complex of stone buildings near Hadrian's Wall that were built to keep Northern hordes at bay. Also, a top secret military facility in the American Midwest

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: To improve their bombs, the US Navy turned to animals for inspiration. However, when 270 men vanished into a cloud, witnesses claimed the soldiers had been abducted by aliens

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Factors influencing America's decision to support Britain during the Second World War, and how Hitler's declaration of war on the US convinced Congress to take more decisive action. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team head to the Royal Hall in Harrogate, with Alastair Dickenson giving his highest valuation in 20 years on the show. Plus, the emotional story of a sapphire ring left to a young man by a recently departed friend, and the family history behind Betty's Tea Rooms

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is in Brighton as he starts the final leg of his journey from Norwich to Chichester, learning about the history of the Royal Pavilion. At Bramber, he discovers that Victorian tourists flocked to the town in huge numbers to visit a museum of taxidermy, before heading into the South Downs for a taste of life in the fast lane at Goodwood

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off from Ayr as he embarks on the first leg of a journey through southern Scotland from west to east, admiring the granite island of Ailsa Craig in the Firth of Clyde before getting to grips with the sport of curling, with a little help from a Scottish world champion. The Ancient Society of Kilwinning Archers invites Michael to take part in a competition, at Barassie he rides the footplate of a freight train hauling coal on Scotland's oldest railway line as he heads on towards Stewarton

programme.title: World War Two From Above
programme.desc: Using drone mounted radar, experts uncover a Second World War German trench system revealing a daring allied paratrooper raid to capture it

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: The story behind the Krupp company, who were responsible for the manufacture of two of Germany's most feared war products - the Panzer Tank and the U-boat

programme.title: Steam Train Britain
programme.desc: On the Welsh Highland Railway, a newly built Pullman carriage is on its way to Caernarfon Station and a Saddle Tank Loco is being completely restored on the East Lancashire Railway

programme.title: The Two Ronnies
programme.desc: Vintage comedy, including part six of Death Can Be Fatal, and the Meadowfield Players put on Weekend in Mayfair

programme.title: The Two Ronnies
programme.desc: Vintage comedy with Messrs Barker and Corbett, including another episode of Death Can Be Fatal

programme.title: Shipwreck Secrets
programme.desc: The discovery of a wreck off the coast of Florida sends a team of underwater explorers on a mission to discover the identity of a ship that simply shouldn't exist

programme.title: Shipwreck Secrets
programme.desc: In 1925 the SS Cotopaxi carrying coal and 32 men vanished. It has since become synonymous with one of the ocean's greatest mysteries - the Bermuda Triangle

programme.title: Princess Margaret: The Rebel Royal
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary profile of the Queen's sister, whose life and loves reflected the social and sexual revolution that transformed Britain during the 20th century. With archive footage and interviews, the series follows Margaret's life as she redefined the image of the modern princess, and begins with her birth in 1930. The abdication of her uncle propelled Margaret to second in line to the throne, but her life would be shaped by dramatic social change as Britain entered a more egalitarian era after the Second World War

programme.title: Princess Margaret: The Rebel Royal
programme.desc: Part two of two. Experts analyse the Princess's marriage to Lord Snowdon at the start of the 1960s. At home with celebrities and artists, they're riding the wave of a cultural and sexual revolution that's transforming Britain. The mystique of monarchy is being increasingly undermined by the emergence of a more open and egalitarian society

programme.title: Royal Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: The crime documentary continues, shedding light on rumours that Princess Anastasia, daughter of Tsar Nicholas II, managed to escape death at the hands of the Bolsheviks in 1918

programme.title: Underground Worlds
programme.desc: A look at some of Switzerland's 300,000 subterranean bunkers, built across history. Plus, the extraordinary creation of a Liverpool entrepreneur that's shrouded in mystery, and a network of man-made tunnels used by adventurous subterranean thrill-seekers deep beneath Slovenia's landscape

programme.title: Underground Worlds

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off from Motherwell as he continues his journey through southern Scotland, celebrating the Victorian iron and steel industry and admires one of its crowning achievements - the Forth Rail Bridge. programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A 1970 Excalibur Roadstar, once owned by Roy Orbison, arrives at Thornton-le-Dale and Derek heads to County Durham to collect a 1935 Austin 7 Ulster

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave's son Jack heads to Cheshire to collect two cars with a 35-year age difference, and luckily for Mathewsons, Lucy knows exactly what's been brought in for auction

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: A hole appears at the bottom of the garden, causing Victor to fly into a rage - much to the alarm of his long-suffering wife. Comedy, starring Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave

To impress Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith, the celebrities serve up millionaire shortbread, a fruity tart and their pet hates in cake form in the showstopper

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 21-year-old patient is brought in with a suspected stroke, which the consultant finds concerning for someone so young. A patient in urgent care is treated for a cut on his leg, and a man breaks his leg after being thrown from his bike. As the patients undergo treatment, they and their families reflect on the role relationships between parent and child have played in their lives

programme.title: Our Baby: A Modern Miracle
programme.desc: Documentary following transgender couple Hannah and Jake Graf as they start a family. Filmed over the course of a year, the programme follows Hannah and Jake from their first meeting with a surrogate through to their baby's birth in the midst of lockdown. The film also explores their experiences of transitioning and the prejudice they've faced, as they strive to realise their dream of becoming parents

programme.title: Extraordinary Escapes with Sandi Toksvig
programme.desc: The presenter explores the UK's wildest, most remote and beautifully designed holiday homes, accompanied by some of the nation's most fascinating and funny women. In the final episode, Sandi is joined by her close friend and The Great British Bake Off judge Prue Leith to explore some of the best staycations in the Cotswolds. The pair visit a luxury tree house, a striking home made from glass, and colourful and chic home designed by Kate Moss

programme.title: The Great Pottery Throw Down
programme.desc: Siobhán McSweeney hosts the semi-final of the pottery contest, in which the four remaining contestants face their biggest challenge to date. They must make a decorative chamber pot and a fully functional pedestal sink, before judges Keith Brymer Jones and Rich Miller decide which three potters will be going through to the final

programme.title: Drawers Off
programme.desc: Management consultant Jon models for the group, but it remains to be seen whether they can handle the surprise he has in store for them. Presented by Jenny Eclair

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: Two years after launching a brewery in France, an Essex couple may have become victims of their own success. The owners of a boat charter business in Alicante have a new plan

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Craig and his daughter Holly are pooling their resources to buy a property in their favourite holiday destination - Majorca. They love the beach and the great outdoors and want to be as close to the coast as possible. With a budget of £180,000, Danni Menzies sets out to find their ideal property

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Roberta Smithies from the Lake District in Cumbria hopes to wave goodbye to her childhood home and embrace a new life in Costa Rica. She has a budget of £200,000 and hopes Jonnie Irwin will find her a property with views that are impressive enough to tempt her to make an offer

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Each year Mary Anderson likes to avoid the UK's cold winter months by spending her time in Naples in Florida, and now wishes to secure her own property in the region. Sara Damergi helps her identify suitable premises, which include a house with its own pool and a beach apartment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Make-up artist Craig Alexander Wilson hosts the first party in Glasgow, hoping his edible glitter and sparklers will impress his fellow diners. However, his guests are less than overawed when he tries to guess their ages, hampering his chances of winning the £1,000 prize at the end of the week

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Paul Cowan hosts the second dinner party in Glasgow, but his penchant for practical jokes threatens to overshadow his menu, which includes steak Balmoral with whisky sauce and the enigmatically named Paul's Cream Puff

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Mandy Wong hopes to impress her guests at the third dinner party in Glasgow with an Asian cuisine-inspired menu. Unfortunately for her, Caroline Wardrope has to run to the bathroom during the host's Latin dance routine, and a disastrous dessert further hampers her bid for success

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Music teacher and professional flautist Siobhan Daly hosts the fourth dinner party challenge in Glasgow, and plans to impress her guests with a rock and pop icons-themed evening. She begins by preparing a cod and chorizo stew before showing off her piano skills for dessert

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Caroline Wardrope hosts the final dinner party in Glasgow, and throws a 1970s-themed disco evening with a mystery menu. The diners seem to enjoy the surprise dishes and are shocked to learn the truth about the host's secret profession, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to Philadelphia to help failing Mexican restaurant Zocalo. Owners Greg and Mary Russell met and fell in love while they were working there, and the place held such sentimental importance to them they pooled their money and bought it. Now Gordon tries to turn things around before the floundering business ruins their marriage and tears their family apart

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman shows five properties to recently retired West Sussex couple Alison and Martin, who are looking to buy a holiday home along Spain's Costa Dorada

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Scarlette Douglas helps Michelle and David from Liverpool search for a holiday home on Spain's Costa del Sol with a budget of £60,000

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: George meets Dai Saunders, who plans to lure his children away from their gadgets by getting them to help build a camouflaged crafting workshop at the end of the garden. In Derbyshire, 14-year-old architect Fran is using her savings to create a hangout den in an old horsebox, and in Tokyo George finds a tiny house that turns out to be pretty big. There is also a visit to the Sussex countryside and a tree-house that hovers among the trees

programme.title: Grayson's Art Club
programme.desc: Grayson and Philippa Perry are joined by actor Russell Tovey, who makes an artwork on the theme of the week - which is nature - and talks about his passion for contemporary art. Grayson and Russell also choose their favourites from the public submissions to this episode. The Turner Prize-winner also talks to celebrated land artist Andy Goldsworthy, who's been creating art during lockdown on his farm in Scotland, and to Polly Morgan, who is renowned for her artworks that feature taxidermy

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: The charity version of the popular baking show returns, and with Noel Fielding on paternity leave, Matt Lucas is presenting on his own. In previous editions, comedian Tom Allen has stepped in to co-present - but today he is showing off his baking skills as one of the contestants, alongside Star Wars actress Daisy Ridley, comedian Rob Beckett and singer Alexandra Burke. To impress Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith, the celebrities serve up millionaire shortbread, a fruity tart and their pet hates in cake form in the showstopper

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, focusing on The Late Late Show with James Corden and Prince Harry, Married at First Sight Australia, Drawers Off, Million Pound Pawn, Your Honor, Pooch Perfect, Stand Up and Deliver, and ITV news of Donald Trump's speech

programme.title: The Sex Clinic
programme.desc: Student Taps comes to the clinic as he has never been able to ejaculate during sex, and he's hoping that the resident therapist will help him overcome his problem. Chloe from Cardiff wants advice on 'pegging', and personal trainer Tracy is concerned that she might be addicted to sex. Finally, barman Tom needs an STI test and Jordan is worried about a lump on his bum

programme.title: The Big Fat Quiz of Everything
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig, Joe Lycett, Katherine Ryan, Big Narstie, David Baddiel and Frank Skinner join quizmaster Jimmy Carr as he poses questions from the last 100,000 years of human history, giving a unique Big Fat Quiz spin on subjects such as history, music, film, TV and science from every epoch. As always, Jimmy is helped by a whole host of celebrity question-setters, including newsreader Jon Snow and actor Charles Dance

programme.title: Stand Up & Deliver
programme.desc: Part two of two. With just 24 hours to go until their comedy debut, the celebrities get some last-minute tips from their mentors. Judi Love coaches Curtis Pritchard in improvisation, Nick Helm teaches Sayeeda Warsi how to make a strong first impression, and Jason Manford is still struggling to get Shaun Ryder to remember his act. Meanwhile, Katie McGlynn tries to harness her nerves and Richard Coles remains reverent when mentor David Baddiel pushes on him a joke with two swear words in the same sentence

programme.title: Luxury Holidays: How to Get Away This Year
programme.desc: Sabrina Grant investigates whether a proper break in 2021 is possible, and where keen holiday-makers can visit once restrictions are lifted. She meets families who are desperate for an escape and explores the options for taking a luxury trip both at home and abroad - from high-end British destinations that are off the beaten track, to cruises and foreign countries that may be open to tourists later in the year

programme.desc: Alex, who drives a £40,000 Mercedes-Benz car, goes to stay with Jen, Antony and baby Jaxon, who survive on £1,000 a month in Bolton

programme.title: Filthy Britain SOS
programme.desc: Newbie Chloe is taken under the wing of experienced enforcers Ian and Anneil as they conduct a street sweep, deep cleaning Hounslow's problem areas

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Claire and Simon seek eviction specialist Chris's help to evict a man claiming to be renting their old family home. Meanwhile, Hamza hopes to get his council flat back after he tried to help a neighbour's friend by letting them stay for a week, but ended up being shut out when they refused to leave. Plus, a man is almost £30,000 in debt and in danger of repossession after his tenants stopped paying the rent

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Cases include Wendy, who was left with rental arrears of almost £3,000 after renting a house out to a nightmare tenant. Steve from Birmingham Council's housing enforcement team investigates poor living conditions above a local shop. And in south London, lawyer Chris has to deal with his client's site which has been filled by nearly two dozen caravans and a mountain of rubbish

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents Stewart and Iain head to Manchester to recover one of the largest debts they have ever chased - more than £400,000 - owed to a builder after he was failed to receive payment for his work on a house. Meanwhile, officers Gary and Cona are in Kent to chase over £11,000 owed by a couple for unpaid rent

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Angus and Sean resort to smashing down a door in a desperate attempt to save a man's life, and Kev and Colin spot a car being driven in the wrong direction down a motorway. Darren encounters a pizza delivery man who is under the influence of alcohol, while out on patrol, Hayley observes the damage that can be caused by Cumbria's treacherously icy roads

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Officers patrol during Black Eye Friday - one of Cumbria's most alcohol-fuelled nights of the year, and help a woman trapped inside a burning car. Meanwhile, a boy racer is made to call his mother after being caught misbehaving, and a motorway pursuit is brought to an abrupt end

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Officers Hayley and Ben can barely believe their eyes when they encounter a drunk driver in the fast lane of the M6, and dog handler Glenn is given a crash course in marine rescue when he answers an emergency distress call. Plus, a monster-truck owner tries to give police the slip and a pizza-delivery driver gets a special order from the interceptors

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Brian and Graham have been warned to expect trouble as they evict some tenants from a south London flat, one of whom has a reputation for violence. Everything goes smoothly until the enforcement agents make a shocking discovery in a child's bedroom. Paul and Steve head to Slough to collect wages owed to a freelance worker, but the debtor is less than happy to find the high-court enforcement officers on his doorstep and things get physical

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Sheriff Paul Bohill tackles the case of a small debt owed by ex-footballer Neil 'Razor' Ruddock to a kennel owner, before being forced to evict a school dinner lady in south London. Meanwhile, in the north of England enforcement agent Stewart MacCracken heads out with trainee Iain Taylor to confront a Yorkshire builder they need to get payment from at his home, while a reptile shop owner hit by online competitors urgently needs to pay a debt for the feed company he relies on to keep his animals alive

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Horrified by her mistakes, Roxy decides to lay low. Bea finally forgives Levi, after considering his strong moral compass. Paul takes action in order to avoid Melanie

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Bella finally enters Colby's old room, but struggles with the idea of taking over her brother's space. Nikau introduces his stepcousin Chloe to Alf

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: Colin and Lizzy Whiting, who find themselves in the wealthiest 10 percent of Britain, swap their five-bedroom house near Newquay to spend a week living in a two-bed property where Sarah and Ross Timmins live. The Timmins on the other hand, are among the poorest 10 percent in the country, and survive on a tenth of the weekly budget the Withings have

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.desc: Graeme Hall comes to the aid of a German Shepherd working as a security dog at a nightclub, who gets confused about who exactly he is supposed to protect the business against. Other clients include a labradoodle who objects to her owners doing any housework and a dispute between the dogs belonging to two generations of the same family

programme.title: Cats and Dogs at War
programme.desc: Chirag and Nanci break up the fights between a cat and a puppy belonging to a Devon couple, and try to control a dog in the Peak District that chases everything

programme.title: The Teacher with Tourette's
programme.desc: The extraordinary story of Natalie, a teacher with a difference - her Tourette's means lessons may be interrupted by uncontrollable swearing and the sort of comments one would never expect to hear in a classroom. The condition was triggered by a traumatic event in her past and while it colours every aspect of her life, she is determined to prove it is no barrier to succeeding at her job. But as she wrestles with new challenges, not to mention the constant stigma and judgement of others, can she overcome the obstacles the faces on a daily basis?

programme.title: Adults Only
programme.desc: Porn star couple Mike and Jess shoot a daring new video and uber cammer Princess Levi has a wild birthday party. Mistress Sofia and daughter Amy prepare for a spanking Halloween party, while 19-year-old escort Ellie has recently taken time off work and needs to reel her online clients back in and restore brand loyalty before other cammers have muscled in and signed them up

programme.title: Ambulance: Code Red
programme.desc: A West Midlands critical care team is called to a road traffic accident in which a 13-year-old boy has been knocked over on the way to school, and now has blood coming out of one ear

programme.title: A&E After Dark
programme.desc: Two people arrive after jumping from an upstairs window during a house fire and consultant Andy must manage their care and ensure that Resus runs smoothly as other serious cases arrive. programme.title: Filthy Britain SOS

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Alex is hit with a £7,000 bill after his tenant is found to have been running a business from his property. Developer Jon calls in an eviction specialist to rid a disused tram station of squatters, and Vicki is surprised to find out that a tenant has stopped paying rent and moved in a sub-tenant

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Watford builder David Simpson has a fight on his hands when his long-term tenant moves her boyfriend in and suddenly decides to stop paying - and when he tries to evict her, she makes life difficult. In Woolwich, lawyer Chris Sharpe helps a client whose property has been taken over by squatters, who have changed the locks and claim they are legitimately renting the house

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Gareth and Mitch are on the trail of £2,500 in unpaid parking tickets in Cardiff, and while the debtor is not at home, they notice his car is sporting false number plates and call the police. The duo also face an angry defendant in Swindon when trying to collect £2,000 for unpaid rent on a lock-up, while in Buckinghamshire, Matt and Garry consider seizing a haul of luxury watches to pay more than £16,000 owed to a landlord

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A huge haul of cash is uncovered during a routine stop, leaving Duggie speechless, and a van driver tries to ram an interceptor on the busy M6. Meanwhile, Hayley and Ben rush to a frightening accident on the motorway, dog-handler Barry catches a burglar and the air ambulance is scrambled to a crash involving a school bus on a country road

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Hayley rushes to help two badly injured motorcyclists, while Ross and John haul in a drink-driver who has left a trail of destruction in his wake. Duncan has to escort a 176-wheel monster truck through Workington, Steve deals with a boy racer, Tom goes undercover on the trail of some suspected bike thieves, and Duggie plays gooseberry on a couple's first date

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Andy collars a suspect thought to have caused criminal damage at a house party, but the father of the host arrives and inflames the situation, while Duggie and Mark respond to a report of a car that has been abandoned after being involved in a crash near Carlisle. In Barrow-in-Furness, the Tactical Support Group calls on Barry and his dog Lucky to sniff out some evidence after a drugs raid

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Enforcement officers are faced with the difficult job of evicting a woman with two children - a task that takes a turn for the worse when her ex-partner makes an appearance. In Liverpool, locals threaten a riot over an unpaid debt at a computer shop, while two agents come across an extremely rare car in the course of their duties

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Paul and Steve discover a locked wooden case when they are called to evict tenants from a flat in London's Docklands. Opening it up, they realise they urgently need police back-up - and the property quickly becomes a crime scene. Brian and Graham head to Swindon Town FC to collect a debt, while Paul's team arrives at a property in Essex to find that they have to remove a woman and her six children

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Sheila manages to talk Roxy out of leaving, and Amy shocks Dipi with a decision to open a business, although Shane could not be happier

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Alf decides to track down Kieran, before ending up in an explosive fight. Jasmine is shocked by the news of Colby's alleged second murder

programme.title: Britain's Parking Hell
programme.desc: The documentary reveals how the problem of finding somewhere to park has caused many motorists to turn selfish on the roads. Traffic wardens struggle to control the chaos in the seaside town of Tenby as drivers head to the coast for a break, and impatient motorists get in the way of a Remembrance Day march and also block the emergency services in the Midlands

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Police officers monitor events as thousands of protesters wearing V for Vendetta masks march to the Houses of Parliament

programme.title: Born to Kill?
programme.desc: Canadian couple Paul Bernardo and Karla Homolka appeared to epitomise love's young dream when they were married, but they were serial killers who raped and murdered three teenage girls - one of them Homolka's 15-year-old sister Tammy. This film delves into their backgrounds and talks to those who knew them in an effort to find out what turned them into killers, while detectives, journalists and criminal psychologists also offer an insight into their motives

programme.title: Watchmen (2009)
programme.desc: At the height of the Cold War, former superheroes driven into retirement by public outcry are confronted with the murder of one of their number. As they face the possibility of a conspiracy to eliminate them, they must contend with a world edging closer to the brink of nuclear war. Sci-fi drama, based on Alan Moore and Dave Gibbons' graphic novel, starring Patrick Wilson, Jackie Earle Haley, Malin Akerman and Billy Crudup

programme.title: Filthy Britain SOS
programme.desc: A dodgy scrap dealer is spotted up to no good in County Durham, but his attempt to flee the grime scene is captured in evidentially invaluable technicolour. In Surrey, a pair of water engineers head out to tackle the damage caused to the UK's sewer system by some of the 11 billion litres of effluent flushed down British U-bends every day, while Malvern's enforcement officers Dan and Jude spend their days hunting for fly-tippers and rubbish dumpers, but need to get their prevention message across in a different way

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: The owner of a tanning salon regrets letting an employee move into a house he owns when he is faced with complaints from the neighbours about wild parties and £3,000 in unpaid rent. A couple managing a property for their daughter arrange to sell it, only to find the tenant refuses to leave, while a housing enforcement officer goes in search of a landlord who has left a tenant without light, heat or running water

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Teorin and Collette had high hopes when they let out their five-bedroom family home, but it wasn't long before their tenant stopped paying rent - and then turned the house into a hostel for holiday-makers. Quickly running out of options, the couple turn to eviction specialist Chris Sharpe for help. Plus, Harrow council officers discover 40 people crammed into a potential deathtrap of a house, and a woman who relies on her rent for medical expenses tries to claw back some from her tenant

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Stewart and Elmor are in Manchester to recover £4,000 owed to a firm of architects, only for the debtor to attempt to throw them off the scent. Stewart is joined by Vic in Liverpool to track down a landlord who owes his former tenants deposits totalling £5,000, while Gareth and Mitch are in for a surprise when they chase a builder in Wiltshire

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The team have their hands full when a raid reveals a crowbar-wielding suspect with a house full of drugs, a Taser and an offensive mouth

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Jon puts his body on the line as he tackles a drunken youth in a domestic dispute and Martin turns detective to catch a drink-driver who thinks she can get one over on the interceptors. A pizza delivery driver take fast food to the extreme, Doug gets to grips with a burglar and the hunt is on for Lincoln's answer to Harry Houdini

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Sgt Carlin arrests a gang of van thieves and uncovers a major criminal operation in the process, while a late-night compost thief is caught on his way out of the shower. Officers hunt for a trucker wielding a baseball bat who has apparently succumbed to road rage and vegetarian Craggy rides to the rescue of a lorry load of calves on the A46. Elsewhere, Brian cracks down on a barbaric blood sport, Jon faces an unexploded bomb, and two fuel thieves plough straight into the interceptors' precious BMW

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court enforcement officers Paul, Steve and Ben make a shocking discovery when they break into a home in Essex while serving an eviction notice on a single mother and her two children. Meanwhile, Stewart and Iain receive more than just threats from the staff and owner of a taxi business, a car dealer has to choose between paying up or losing his showroom stars, and an Indian restaurant owner is in danger of losing his livelihood

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court enforcement officers Paul Bohill and Steve and Ben Pinner face their biggest eviction yet - taking on squatters in a large central London office block. With the help of the police, they try to remove a suspected group of 70 from the building, but with a barricade in place, the team faces a challenging operation ahead. Elsewhere, officers are confronted by an army of family and friends when they attempt to enforce a writ at the property of a debtor, and face a difficult eviction at the north London home of a heavily pregnant tenant and her family, after the council fails to pay the rent to the landlord

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Dipi is forced to face the consequences of her actions. Hendrix, unable to take a hint, arranges another horribly misguided romantic gesture towards Harlow

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Jasmine distracts herself with exercise, while Justin and Leah realise they have only one day to prepare an engagement party for Christian and Tori

programme.title: Flights from Hell
programme.desc: Footage of shocking and life-threatening incidents on board aircraft, including an emergency that was declared on a flight from London to Oslo when one of the engines burst into flames. Plus, the pilot of a flight from New York to Warsaw is forced to land the plane with no landing gear, with Polish news capturing the incredible moment the aircraft headed down toward the ground to perform a 'belly flop' onto the runway

programme.title: Inside McDonalds: The 300 Billion Dollar Burger
programme.desc: The history of the fast food chain, examining its origins and its spread to become an international company, with outlets opening in Britain in the 1970s and even Russia embracing the franchise in 1990. The film also examines the controversies that beset the company in the 1990s, with scandals concerning unhealthy eating, advertising to children and animal welfare

programme.title: The Green Mile (1999)
programme.desc: A convicted child-killer of towering height and massive build is condemned to death row, but astonishes the guards with his good nature and miraculous healing powers. They believe the gentle giant is incapable of crime, and emotions run high as they realise there is no hope of overturning his death sentence. Supernatural drama, based on a novel by Stephen King, starring Tom Hanks, Michael Clarke Duncan, David Morse and Harry Dean Stanton

Starring Angela Griffin, Shenae Grimes-Beech and Wendy Crewson

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Ducky is attacked by a knife-wielding woman who accuses him of war crimes, alleging he tortured and killed her brother in Afghanistan in the early 1980s. Gibbs investigates and ascertains what really happened - but has to work with the Afghan embassy to avoid an international incident. Drama, starring David McCallum

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents investigate the private life of a murdered Navy captain whose actions may have led to his death, while McGee finds his ideal woman on the internet - but it turns out she is too good to be true. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray, Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A legal-aid strike leads first to murder, and then to Rubirosa pitting her wits against Cutter as the ADA tries to bring a case against a pro golfer accused of the killing of a paralegal. American legal drama, starring Alana De La Garza and Linus Roache

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate the world of online romance when a garage mechanic is murdered, but the case takes a series of bizarre turns before they are able to identify the killer. Police drama, starring Anthony Anderson and Jeremy Sisto

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The body of a drug addict is discovered in the yard of a daycare centre, and evidence reveals she was the heiress to the fortune. However, suspicions are raised when the family's lawyer gets power of attorney over the trust fund. Police drama, starring Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate when lawyers Fred and Lizzy Bellamy are found murdered in their sleep. The case leads them to two couples who are in the process of adopting children from Haiti, but the detectives uncover signs of abuse that point to a human-trafficking ring

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate when a plaintiff involved in a high-profile lawsuit against airline Pan World is found dead in his car. The detectives soon discover the case had been delayed after the presiding judge was murdered and the clues lead to a lawyer willing to do anything for money

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: An unfinished building is demolished when a bomb is detonated on-site, killing a 12-year-old boy. Logan and Briscoe call on the construction company, and their suspicions fall on a bankrupt contractor and a jealous husband. Police drama, starring Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Inquiries into the death of a marine land the team members squarely in the middle of an inner-city gang war - and a message found at the scene of the crime seemingly implicates Gibbs. Forced to confront a secret from his past, he calls his mentor Mike Franks for help. Drama, guest starring Muse Watson and Kari Coleman, with Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: One of Tony's old murder investigations is reopened and, in an attempt to find the perpetrator, he decides to switch roles with Gibbs, leading the team in an inquiry that links the case to an embezzlement scheme. American drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Sasha Alexander, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team heads into the Arizona desert to locate an artist who might be able to help solve the murder of fellow agent Jack Patterson - and discovers one of her paintings holds the key to the mystery. Meanwhile, DiNozzo learns he is in line for an inheritance

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: After a mural by a street artist and activist is stolen, the investigation leads to a conspiracy regarding a Navy contractor and the safety of ocean mammals. American drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum and Sean Murray

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: In an effort to find the terrorist Parsa, Gibbs and the NCIS team track down one of his cohorts using evidence gleaned from the drone attack which has killed six people and wounded a further 22. Meanwhile, an emotionally shaken McGee struggles with the ramifications of the incident. Drama, starring Mark Harmon, Sean Murray, David McCallum and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The murder of a port authority officer reveals Parsa's possible whereabouts as the team continues the hunt for the terrorist intent on wiping out NCIS. Mark Harmon and Emily Wickersham star

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The team sets out to track down a serial rapist, and traces the culprit to a homeless shelter, where the trail goes cold. programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The team tries to prevent a miscarriage of justice after a young model is raped by a renowned fashion photographer, who looks to his successful brother to make the charges disappear. Crime drama, starring Mariska Hartigay

programme.desc: An abortion clinic doctor is murdered, and when Logan and Briscoe investigate, they begin to suspect members of a radical movement led by a former priest, who appears to have no sympathy for the man's fate. Starring Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach

programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate when Harold and Joyce Foley arrive home to find their son and housekeeper murdered. The detectives start to believe the couple's work on a controversial pharmaceutical study made them targets, until further evidence changes their minds

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate when a trucking company owner is found murdered. programme.title: Law & Order

Crime drama, starring Pauley Perrette and Mark Harmon

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A contestant on a reality dating show claims to have been raped. Co-operating with the SVU, the programme's producers agree to let Benson and her team go undercover on the set for an incognito investigation. Crime drama, starring Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Hyacinth Miller from London first visited Barbados 14 years ago, and now that she's retired, wants to permanently relocate there and spend time in the garden. With her son Leon along to help choose the right property, Jasmine Harman shows Hyacinth five properties in the south of the island, but do any of them have her packing her bags for a new life in the sun?

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: A man is caught on camera abusing a dog, and a horse needs help after collapsing

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: Chief Investigator Boller visits a livestock auction, where he rescues horses from slaughter by outbidding buyers from meat markets

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees
programme.desc: A dangerous rescue mission is launched to secure two dogs spotted running wild on train tracks. Plus, a couple arrive at the Villalobos Rescue Centre looking to adopt a dog

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: A horse with overgrown teeth is seized, and an elderly woman hides her dogs from investigators

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore transforms items reclaimed from a recycling centre in Walsall, West Midlands, featuring a pair of chairs, a plant stand and printed bean sacks

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and Philip Serrell visit the antiques shops of Yorkshire, along the way meeting a trombone-maker and discovering the history of the rocking horse

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and Philip Serrell scour the antiques shops around Sheffield to find something that will give them the edge at the auction. Natasha goes out of her comfort zone when considering some 18th-century pottery, but feels more at home when she spots something from the 1970s. Philip opts for some typically 'Serrell' items, as well as chewing over more prehistoric stuff. He also goes out of his way to prove that chivalry isn't dead. On the road, Philip visits a factory using the world-famous Sheffield steel to make some very fine tools, and Natasha heads to the woods for a spot of foraging and some free food. It's going to be a nail biter at their final auction at Lytham St Annes, Lancashire

programme.title: My Feet Are Killing Me
programme.desc: Dr Vincent performs a delicate surgery when she treats a toddler born with 12 toes. Plus, things get tense when a patient wakes up in the middle of their operation

programme.title: Embarrassing Bodies
programme.desc: Pixie McKenna investigates new surgery techniques involving the anus, and helps a man suffering with hot testicles. Dawn Harper meets a woman who had a stroke at just 45, revealing whether electrotherapy can help her regain more of her bodily functions, and investigates the worrying trend of buying legal highs online. Christian Jessen is joined by Nurse Joy Tickle to help treat assorted skin conditions for visitors in Birmingham, and in Magaluf there's a look at the serious side effects of spiking someone's drink

programme.title: Salvage Hunters: The Restorers
programme.desc: The artisan restorers behind Drew Pritchard's antiques empire save an iconic Eames lounge chair. They also revive an unusual 90-year old oil lamp and a glamourous steam trunk

programme.title: Ghost Adventures
programme.desc: Zak Bagans, Nick Groff and Aaron Goodwin explore a former psychiatric facility in Illinois, which was opened as a haunted house after being abandoned for almost 20 years

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: The team visits the Ancient High House in Stafford, supposedly haunted by long-dead spirits. Gregg feels as if something is playing games with him

Dr Pixie McKenna is also on hand to reveal the truth about detox diets

programme.desc: A taxi driver is the victim of an April Fools' Day prank when he is called out to a derelict area - but when an eight-year-old girl is later murdered at the site, he is wrongly charged with the crime. A lawyer is moved by the defendant's wife to represent him, only to face a prosecution case made by his own father. Crime drama, starring Richard Attenborough and Cathy O'Donnell

programme.desc: The leaders of eight rival noble houses gather for a festival designed to settle their long-standing feuds once and for all. programme.desc: Gary goes to the pub to drown his sorrows, so Dermot invites Deborah to their flat for a romantic evening of candlelight, dinner and music. Laddish comedy, starring Harry Enfield and Martin Clunes

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Dermot is sacked for dangling a customer by the ankles, while Gary, on the other hand, is offered a new post. However, he has difficulty breaking the news to George and Anthea. Laddish comedy, starring Harry Enfield and Martin Clunes

programme.title: The Old Guys
programme.desc: Roy visits Amber's therapist to help raise his spirits, and ends up revealing fantasies about his neighbour Sally, whom the counsellor then meets at a dinner party, where a mutual attraction quickly develops, urging Tom and Roy to take desperate measures to keep them apart. Comedy, starring Clive Swift and Roger Lloyd Pack

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tour exploring Fitzrovia for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: The Ronnie Wood Show
programme.desc: The Rolling Stones guitarist is joined by Stereophonics frontman Kelly Jones, who discusses some of his favourite songs, including Otis Redding's Cigarettes and Coffee and Who Killed Davey Moore? by Bob Dylan

programme.title: Once a Soldier
programme.desc: Open for over 300 years and home to 320 old soldiers, the Royal Hospital in Chelsea is under threat. This series follows the exceptional men for a year of their lives

programme.title: The Queen's Cavalry
programme.desc: The trainee troopers prepare for their final riding exam over one of the Army's toughest courses. Originally hopeless horseman Glenn is apprehensive - as is Adrian Van Loon, whose kit often fails to meet the rigorous standards. Gunners from the armoured regiment practise on the firing range with live ammunition, and the Household Cavalry's historian talks about their role in the battle of Waterloo

programme.title: EFL Cup Highlights
programme.desc: Manchester United v Tottenham Hotspur. Action from the showpiece encounter at Wembley Stadium, where the first major trophy of the season was contested. Spurs were the holders, having defeated Chelsea 2-1 after extra time in last year's final, while the Red Devils had last lifted the cup in 2006 following a comfortable 4-0 win against Wigan Athletic

programme.title: EFL Cup Highlights
programme.desc: Liverpool v Cardiff City. Action from the showpiece match at Wembley Stadium, where the winner of the first major trophy of the 2011/12 season was decided. The Reds had defeated the likes of Stoke City, Chelsea and Manchester City to reach the final and went into the match as clear favourites in their first visit to the national stadium since it was rebuilt. However, the Bluebirds, who defeated Crystal Palace on penalties in the semi-finals, had been in good form in the Championship and will have fancied their chances of producing an upset

programme.title: EFL Cup Highlights
programme.desc: Bradford City v Swansea City. Highlights from Wembley Stadium in 2013, where the League Two Bantams were aiming to produce yet another cup upset. Swansea were appearing in their first major cup final and beat Chelsea in the semi-finals to reach this stage, but Bradford had stolen most of the headlines in the competition this season, belying their lowly league status to eliminate the likes of Arsenal, Aston Villa and Wigan Athletic

programme.title: Live MNF
programme.desc: West Ham United v Leeds United (Kick-off 8.00pm). Coverage of the top-flight clash from The London Stadium. West Ham recorded a 2-1 away win when the sides met at Elland Road in December, with Tomas Soucek and Angelo Ogbonna's goals turning the match around after Mateusz Klich had put Leeds in front from the penalty spot. Leeds haven't beaten the Hammers away from home since a 4-3 victory back in November 2002.

programme.title: The Players Championship Live
programme.desc: Build up to this year's Championship, as the tournament returns for the first time in full since 2019, after it was cancelled after the first round due to the coronavirus pandemic. Rory McIlroy will be looking to defend the competition he won two years ago, after he finished one stroke clear of Jim Furyk with a score of 16-under-par. He was the first European to win this tournament for five years after German Martin Kaymer's win in 2014.

programme.title: The Players Championship Live
programme.desc: Build up to this year's Championship, as the tournament returns for the first time in full since 2019, after it was cancelled after the first round due to the coronavirus pandemic. Rory McIlroy will be looking to defend the competition he won two years ago, after he finished one stroke clear of Jim Furyk with a score of 16-under-par. He was the first European to win this tournament for five years after German Martin Kaymer's win in 2014.

programme.title: Live MNF
programme.desc: West Ham United v Leeds United (Kick-off 8.00pm). Coverage of the top-flight clash from The London Stadium. West Ham recorded a 2-1 away win when the sides met at Elland Road in December, with Tomas Soucek and Angelo Ogbonna's goals turning the match around after Mateusz Klich had put Leeds in front from the penalty spot. Leeds haven't beaten the Hammers away from home since a 4-3 victory back in November 2002.

programme.desc: Manchester City v Southampton (Kick-off 6.00pm).
